title: Isotropic Average Electron-Phonon Coupling Constant
content: The isotropic average electron-phonon coupling constant $\lambda$ is defined by the Eliashberg spectral function $\alpha^{2}F(\omega)$ through the integral $\lambda = 2\int_{0}^{\infty} d\omega \frac{\alpha^{2}F(\omega)}{\omega}$. This dimensionless quantity $\lambda$ is equivalent to the mass enhancement factor $\lambda(n=0)$ at the Fermi level, where $\lambda(n)$ is the Fermi-surface average of the momentum-dependent coupling $\lambda(\vec{k},\vec{k}^{\prime},n)$.

title: Properties of BaCu3O4 precursor
content: In the synthesis of Tl-based superconductors, $BaCu_{3}O_{4}$ is utilized as a precursor because it possesses the lowest melting point among the various barium-copper oxides ($Ba$-$Cu$-$O$); furthermore, molten $BaCu_{3}O_{4}$ exhibits high fluidity, which promotes more complete melt-solid reactions during synthesis compared to standard solid-state reactions.

extra.notations:
- $Tl_{2}O_{3}$: Thallium(III) oxide
- $CaO$: Calcium oxide
- $BaCu_{3}O_{4}$: Barium copper oxide precursor
- $T_{c}$: Superconducting transition temperature

title: Coulomb Pseudopotential
content: The dimensionless Coulomb pseudopotential $\mu^{*}(\omega)$, representing the retarded Coulomb repulsion between electrons at a frequency scale $\omega$, is defined by the relation $\mu^{*}(\omega)=\frac{\mu}{1+\mu\ln(\epsilon_{F}/\omega)}$, where $\mu$ is the static Coulomb coupling and $\epsilon_{F}$ is the Fermi energy of the system.

- $Z$: Renormalization function or mass enhancement function.

title: Anharmonic Potential Energy Expansion
content:
For the degenerate, in-plane boron-boron stretching phonon modes ($E_{2g}$) at the $\Gamma$ and $A$ points of the Brillouin zone in $MgB_{2}$, the total potential energy $E(r,\theta)$ as a function of the phonon displacement amplitude $r$ and angle $\theta$ is modeled by the polynomial expansion:
\[ E(r,\theta)=E_{0}+c_{2}r^{2}+c_{4}r^{4}+(c_{3}r^{3}+c_{5}r^{5})\cos(3\theta) \]
where $E_{0}$ is the ground state energy and $c_{2}, c_{3}, c_{4}, c_{5}$ are fitting coefficients determined from first-principles total energy calculations.

title: Isotope Effect Correction for Coulomb Pseudopotential
content:
Under the condition that the Eliashberg equation cutoff frequency $\omega_{c}$ is scaled in proportion to the superconducting transition temperature $T_{c}$ (i.e., $\delta\omega_{c}/\omega_{c} = \delta T_{c}/T_{c}$), the resulting first-order variation in the Coulomb pseudopotential $\mu^{*}(\omega_{c})$ is given by the expression:
\[ \delta\mu^{*}(\omega_{c})=\big[\mu^{*}(\omega_{c})\big]^{2}\frac{\delta T_{c}}{T_{c}}. \]

title: MgB2 superconducting transition temperature
content: Magnesium diboride (MgB$_{2}$) is a superconductor with superconducting transition temperature $T_{c} = 39\,$K.

refine_history.reasoning: The original parent combined the single shared experimental fact ($T_{c}=39\,$K) with a long list of theoretical notation and definitions that the child (Node 1321) does not provide. Because every child must individually entail the parent, I removed all notation and definitional claims that are not supported by all children and retained only the substantive intersection: that MgB$_{2}$ is a superconductor with $T_{c}=39\,$K. The revised parent is self-contained, fully entailed by Child 1321, and avoids the previous union error introduced by unsupported notation items.

extra.notations: - $T_{c}$: superconducting transition temperature

title: Assumption: Computational Parameters
content:
The first-principles numerical solution for $MgB_{2}$ assumes the following parameters and methods:
- Electronic structure is calculated using ab initio pseudopotentials within the local-density approximation (LDA).
- The electronic wave functions are expanded in a plane wave basis set with a kinetic energy cutoff of 60 Ry.
- Brillouin zone integration uses a $12\times12\times12$ k-point grid for self-consistent density calculations and a $18\times18\times12$ grid for evaluating Fermi surface properties.
- The Matsubara frequency summation cutoff $\omega_{c}$ is set to 0.5 eV.
- The standard value for the Coulomb pseudopotential at the cutoff is $\mu^{*}(\omega_{c}) = 0.12$.

title: First-principles calculation of superconducting properties
content: Using the fully anisotropic Eliashberg equation with ab initio calculated anharmonic phonon inputs and a standard Coulomb pseudopotential $\mu^{*}(\omega_{c})=0.12$, the authors determine the superconducting transition temperature of $MgB_{2}$ to be $T_{c}=39$ K, the average electron-phonon coupling constant to be $\lambda=0.61$, and the boron isotope-effect exponent to be $\alpha_{B}=0.32$, all of which are in quantitative agreement with experimental measurements.

title: Anisotropic Eliashberg Equations at Transition Temperature
content:
The anisotropic Eliashberg equations for a superconductor at the transition temperature $T_{c}$ are defined by the following coupled equations for the renormalization function $Z(\vec{k},i\omega_{n})$ and the gap function $\Delta(\vec{k},i\omega_{n})$:
$Z(\vec{k},i\omega_{n})=1+f_{n}s_{n}\sum_{\vec{k}^{\prime}n^{\prime}}W_{\vec{k}^{\prime}}\lambda(\vec{k},\vec{k}^{\prime},n-n^{\prime})s_{n^{\prime}}$ and
$Z(\vec{k},i\omega_{n})\Delta(\vec{k},i \omega_{n})=\sum_{\vec{k}^{\prime}n^{\prime}}W_{\vec{k}^{\prime}}f_{n^{\prime}}[\lambda(\vec{k},\vec{k}^{\prime},n-n^{\prime})-\mu^{*}(\omega_{c})]\Delta(\vec{k}^{\prime},i \omega_{n^{\prime}})$.
Here, $\omega_{n}=(2n+1)\pi T_{c}$ are Matsubara frequencies, $f_{n}=1/|2n+1|$, $s_{n}=\omega_{n}/|\omega_{n}|$, $W_{\vec{k}}$ is the fraction of the density of states at wavevector $\vec{k}$ on the Fermi surface, $\lambda(\vec{k},\vec{k}^{\prime},n-n^{\prime})$ is the momentum-dependent electron-phonon coupling, and $\mu^{*}(\omega_{c})$ is the Coulomb pseudopotential at the cutoff frequency $\omega_{c}$.

extra.notations:
- $T_{c}$: superconducting transition temperature
- $i\omega_{n}$: Matsubara frequencies, where $\omega_{n}=(2n+1)\pi T_{c}$ for an integer $n$
- $Z(\vec{k},i\omega_{n})$: anisotropic renormalization function at wavevector $\vec{k}$ and Matsubara frequency $i\omega_{n}$
- $\Delta(\vec{k},i\omega_{n})$: anisotropic superconducting gap function
- $W_{\vec{k}}$: fraction of the electronic density of states at wavevector $\vec{k}$ on the Fermi surface
- $\lambda(\vec{k},\vec{k}^{\prime},n-n^{\prime})$: momentum-dependent electron-phonon coupling function
- $\mu^{*}(\omega_{c})$: dimensionless Coulomb pseudopotential evaluated at cutoff frequency $\omega_{c}$
- $s_{n}$: sign of the Matsubara frequency, defined as $s_{n} = \text{sgn}(\omega_{n})$
- $f_{n}$: frequency factor defined as $f_{n} = 1/|2n+1|$
- $\epsilon_{F}$: Fermi energy
- $\mu$: initial Coulomb coupling constant before renormalization
- $E(r, \theta)$: total energy variation as a function of frozen phonon amplitude $r$ and angle $\theta$

title: Isotropic Eliashberg Equations at Transition Temperature
content:
The isotropic Eliashberg equations at the transition temperature $T_{c}$, which represent a momentum-averaged simplification of the anisotropic formalism, are given by:
$Z(i\omega_{n})=1+f_{n}s_{n}\sum_{n^{\prime}}\lambda(n-n^{\prime})s_{n^{\prime}}$ and
$Z(i\omega_{n})\Delta(i\omega_{n})=\sum_{n^{\prime}}f_{n^{\prime}}[\lambda(n-n^{\prime})-\mu^{*}(\omega_{c})]\Delta(i\omega_{n^{\prime}})$.
In these expressions, $\omega_{n}=(2n+1)\pi T_{c}$, $f_{n}=1/|2n+1|$, $s_{n}=\omega_{n}/|\omega_{n}|$, $\mu^{*}(\omega_{c})$ is the Coulomb pseudopotential at cutoff frequency $\omega_{c}$, and $\lambda(n-n^{\prime})$ is the isotropically averaged electron-phonon coupling.

extra.notations:
- $T_{c}$: superconducting transition temperature
- $i\omega_{n}$: Matsubara frequencies, where $\omega_{n}=(2n+1)\pi T_{c}$ for an integer $n$

- $\Delta(\vec{k},i\omega_{n})$: anisotropic superconducting gap function
- $W_{\vec{k}}$: fraction of the electronic density of states at wavevector $\vec{k}$ on the Fermi surface
- $\lambda(\vec{k},\vec{k}^{\prime},n-n^{\prime})$: momentum-dependent electron-phonon coupling function

- $f_{n}$: frequency factor defined as $f_{n} = 1/|2n+1|$
- $\epsilon_{F}$: Fermi energy
- $\mu$: initial Coulomb coupling constant before renormalization
- $E(r, \theta)$: total energy variation as a function of frozen phonon amplitude $r$ and angle $\theta$

title: Definition of Isotropic Electron-Phonon Coupling Average
content:
The isotropically averaged electron-phonon coupling $\lambda(n)$ is defined as the weighted average of the momentum-dependent coupling $\lambda(\vec{k},\vec{k}^{\prime},n)$ over all pairs of wavevectors $(\vec{k},\vec{k}^{\prime})$ on the Fermi surface:
$\lambda(n)\equiv\sum_{\vec{k}\vec{k}^{\prime}}W_{\vec{k}}W_{\vec{k}^{\prime}}\lambda(\vec{k},\vec{k}^{\prime},n)$,
where $W_{\vec{k}}$ denotes the fraction of the electronic density of states at wavevector $\vec{k}$ on the Fermi surface such that $\sum_{\vec{k}}W_{\vec{k}}=1$.

title: Renormalization of the Coulomb Pseudopotential
content:
The dimensionless Coulomb pseudopotential $\mu^{*}(\omega)$ at a frequency scale $\omega$ is related to the bare Coulomb coupling $\mu$ and the Fermi energy $\epsilon_{F}$ through the renormalization formula:
$\mu^{*}(\omega)=\frac{\mu}{1+\mu\ln(\epsilon_{F}/\omega)}$.

title: Synthesis procedure
content: The procedure for synthesizing bulk Tl-Ca/Ba-Cu-O superconductors involves mixing stoichiometric amounts of $Tl_{2}O_{3}$, $CaO$, and $BaCu_{3}O_{4}$ precursors, grinding and pressing the mixture into a pellet, heating said pellet to a temperature between 880 °C and 910 °C for a duration of 3 to 5 minutes in a flowing oxygen environment, and subsequently quenching the sample to room temperature in air.

extra.notations:
- $Tl_{2}O_{3}$: Thallium(III) oxide
- $CaO$: Calcium oxide
- $BaCu_{3}O_{4}$: Barium copper oxide precursor
- $T_{c}$: Superconducting transition temperature

title: Assumption: Anharmonic Potential Model for In-Plane Phonon Modes
content:
To account for phonon anharmonicity in the degenerate in-plane boron-boron stretching modes ($E_{2g}$) of $MgB_{2}$, the total energy variation $E(r,\theta)$ relative to the equilibrium energy $E_{0}$ as a function of frozen phonon displacement magnitude $r$ and polar angle $\theta$ is modeled using the polynomial expansion:
$E(r,\theta)=E_{0}+c_{2}r^{2}+c_{4}r^{4}+(c_{3}r^{3}+c_{5}r^{5})\cos(3\theta)$,
where $c_{2}, c_{3}, c_{4},$ and $c_{5}$ are fitting coefficients.

title: Assumption: Computational Framework for Electronic and Vibrational Properties
content: The calculation of electronic wave functions, phonon frequencies, and electron-phonon matrix elements is performed using the ab initio pseudopotential density-functional theory (DFT) framework within the local-density approximation (LDA) for the exchange-correlation functional.

title: Crucial role of electron-phonon anisotropy
content: The strong momentum dependence (anisotropy) of the electron-phonon interaction is the primary mechanism responsible for the high $T_{c}$ in $MgB_{2}$; solving the isotropic Eliashberg equation with the same material parameters yields a transition temperature of only $T_{c}=19$ K, demonstrating that isotropic approximations severely underestimate $T_{c}$ in this material.

title: Anisotropic Eliashberg Equation at Transition Temperature
content:
The anisotropic Eliashberg equation at the superconducting transition temperature $T_{c}$ is expressed as a set of coupled linear equations for the renormalization function $Z(\vec{k},i\omega_{n})$ and the gap function $\Delta(\vec{k},i\omega_{n})$:
\[ Z(\vec{k},i\omega_{n})=1+f_{n}s_{n}\sum_{\vec{k}^{\prime}n^{\prime}}W_{\vec{k}^{\prime}}\lambda(\vec{k},\vec{k}^{\prime},n-n^{\prime})s_{n^{\prime}} \]
\[ Z(\vec{k},i\omega_{n})\Delta(\vec{k},i \omega_{n})=\sum_{\vec{k}^{\prime}n^{\prime}}W_{\vec{k}^{\prime}}f_{n^{\prime}}[\lambda(\vec{k},\vec{k}^{\prime},n-n^{\prime})-\mu^{*}(\omega_{c})]\Delta(\vec{k}^{\prime},i \omega_{n^{\prime}}) \]
where $\vec{k}$ and $\vec{k}^{\prime}$ are wave vectors on the Fermi surface, $i\omega_{n}$ are Matsubara frequencies with $\omega_{n}=(2n+1)\pi T_{c}$, $f_{n}=1/|2n+1|$, $s_{n}$ is the sign of $\omega_{n}$, $W_{\vec{k}}$ is the weight of the density of states at $\vec{k}$, $\lambda(\vec{k},\vec{k}^{\prime},n-n^{\prime})$ is the electron-phonon coupling kernel, and $\mu^{*}(\omega_{c})$ is the Coulomb pseudopotential at cutoff frequency $\omega_{c}$.

\[ E(r,\theta)=E_{0}+c_{2}r^{2}+c_{4}r^{4}+(c_{3}r^{3}+c_{5}r^{5})\cos(3\theta) \]

title: Definition of Harmonic and Anharmonic Phonon Frequencies
content: The harmonic phonon frequency of a mode is defined classically using only the quadratic coefficient ($c_{2}$) of the fitted potential energy curve $E(r) \approx E_{0} + c_{2}r^{2}$. The anharmonic phonon frequency is defined as the energy difference between the two lowest quantum-mechanical vibrational eigenstates calculated by solving the Schrödinger equation for a Hamiltonian that includes the full anharmonic potential energy surface (including higher-order terms like $r^{3}$ and $r^{4}$).

title: Isotropic Average Electron-Phonon Coupling Constant
content:
The isotropic average electron-phonon coupling constant $\lambda$ is calculated from the isotropic Eliashberg spectral function $\alpha^{2}F(\omega)$ according to the integral:
\[ \lambda = 2\int_{0}^{\infty} \frac{\alpha^{2}F(\omega)}{\omega} d\omega \]
where $\omega$ represents the phonon frequency.

title: Frequency Dependence of the Coulomb Pseudopotential
content:
The dimensionless Coulomb pseudopotential $\mu^{*}(\omega)$ at a specific frequency $\omega$ is related to the initial Coulomb interaction parameter $\mu$ and the Fermi energy $\epsilon_{F}$ by the renormalization formula:
\[ \mu^{*}(\omega)=\frac{\mu}{1+\mu\ln(\epsilon_{F}/\omega)} \]
where $\epsilon_{F}$ is the electronic Fermi energy of the system.

title: Suppression of superconductivity by phonon anharmonicity
content: Anharmonicity of the lattice vibrations significantly suppresses superconductivity in $MgB_{2}$ by hardening the in-plane B–B stretching mode ($E_{2g}$) frequency from a harmonic value of 62.7 meV to an anharmonic value of 75.9 meV and reducing the average electron-phonon coupling $\lambda$ from 0.73 to 0.61, which lowers the calculated anisotropic $T_{c}$ from 55 K to 39 K.

title: Anisotropic Eliashberg Equation
content:
The superconducting transition temperature $T_c$ and the momentum-dependent gap function $\Delta(\vec{k}, i\omega_{n})$ are determined by the self-consistent solution of the anisotropic Eliashberg equations:
\[ Z(\vec{k},i\omega_{n})=1+f_{n}s_{n}\sum_{\vec{k}^{\prime}n^{\prime}}W_{\vec{k}^{\prime}}\lambda(\vec{k},\vec{k}^{\prime},n-n^{\prime})s_{n^{\prime}} \]
\[ Z(\vec{k},i\omega_{n})\Delta(\vec{k},i \omega_{n})=\sum_{\vec{k}^{\prime}n^{\prime}}W_{\vec{k}^{\prime}}f_{n^{\prime}}[\lambda(\vec{k},\vec{k}^{\prime},n-n^{\prime})-\mu^{*}(\omega_{c})]\Delta(\vec{k}^{\prime},i \omega_{n^{\prime}}) \]
where $\omega_{n}=(2n+1)\pi T_{c}$ are the Matsubara frequencies, $f_{n}=1/|2n+1|$, $s_n$ is the sign of $\omega_n$, $W_{\vec{k}}$ is the weight of the state at $\vec{k}$ on the Fermi surface proportional to the local density of states, $Z$ is the renormalization function, $\lambda(\vec{k},\vec{k}^{\prime},n-n^{\prime})$ is the electron-phonon interaction kernel, and $\mu^{*}(\omega_{c})$ is the Coulomb pseudopotential at the cutoff frequency $\omega_c$.

title: Vegard's volume rule
content: Vegard's volume rule establishes a systematic relationship between the lattice constants (or unit cell volume) of a solid solution and the concentration of its dopants, which allows for the quantitative determination of the dopant content $x$ in a substituted material, such as $F^{-}$ substituted for $O^{2-}$ in $La[O_{1-x}F_{x}]FeAs$, based on measured lattice parameters.

extra.notations:
- $LaOMPn$: A class of quaternary oxypnictides where $La$ is Lanthanum, $O$ is Oxygen, $M$ is a transition metal (Mn, Fe, Co, or Ni), and $Pn$ is a pnictogen (P or As).
- $P4/nmm$: A specific tetragonal space group symmetry.
- $FeAs$: Iron arsenide, which can occur as an impurity phase in the synthesis of iron-based oxypnictides.
- Vegard's volume rule: An empirical heuristic stating that a linear relation exists, at constant temperature, between the crystal lattice parameter of an alloy and the concentrations of the constituent elements.
- $x$: The molar fraction of a dopant (specifically $F^{-}$ substituting for $O^{2-}$ in $La[O_{1-x}F_{x}]FeAs$).

title: Measurement technique
content: The electrical resistance of the synthesized Tl-Ca/Ba-Cu-O superconducting samples is measured as a function of temperature using the standard four-probe technique applied to rectangular bars cut from the prepared pellets, with electrical contacts established using silver paste.

extra.notations:
- $Tl_{2}O_{3}$: Thallium(III) oxide
- $CaO$: Calcium oxide
- $BaCu_{3}O_{4}$: Barium copper oxide precursor
- $T_{c}$: Superconducting transition temperature

title: Potential Energy Surface for Anharmonic Phonons
content:
For the degenerate, in-plane B-B stretching modes ($E_{2g}$) at high-symmetry points $\Gamma$ and $A$ in the Brillouin zone of $MgB_2$, the potential energy surface $E(r,\theta)$ is modeled using a two-dimensional polynomial expansion in the displacement coordinates $r$ and $\theta$:
\[ E(r,\theta)=E_{0}+c_{2}r^{2}+c_{4}r^{4}+(c_{3}r^{3}+c_{5}r^{5})\cos(3\theta) \]
where $E_{0}$ is the equilibrium energy, and $c_2, c_3, c_4, c_5$ are fitting coefficients determined from first-principles total-energy calculations.

title: Correction to Coulomb Pseudopotential for Isotope Effect
content:
In the calculation of the isotope-effect exponent, if the cutoff frequency $\omega_c$ is scaled in linear proportion to the superconducting transition temperature $T_c$ to maintain numerical consistency, the resulting differential change in the Coulomb pseudopotential $\mu^{*}(\omega_{c})$ is defined by the relation:
\[ \delta\mu^{*}(\omega_{c})=\big[\mu^{*}(\omega_{c})\big]^{2}\frac{\delta T_{c}}{T_{c}} \]
where $\delta T_c$ is the change in transition temperature due to isotopic substitution.

title: Assumption: Isotope Effect Definition
content: The isotope-effect exponent $\alpha$ is defined by the power-law scaling relationship between the superconducting transition temperature $T_{c}$ and the atomic mass $M$ of the substituted isotope, expressed as $T_{c} \propto M^{-\alpha}$.

title: Assumption: Computational Framework
content: The underlying electronic band structure, harmonic and anharmonic phonon frequencies, and the electron-phonon matrix elements $g_{k,k'}^{j}$ are derived from ab initio pseudopotential density-functional theory (DFT) calculations performed within the local-density approximation (LDA).

title: Origin of the reduced isotope effect
content: The anomalously low boron isotope-effect exponent $\alpha_{B}=0.32$ (compared to the harmonic value of $\approx 0.46$) is primarily caused by phonon anharmonicity, which weakens the electron-phonon interaction, rather than by electromagnetic or other exotic mechanisms.

title: Definition of linear electron-phonon matrix elements
content: The linear electron-phonon matrix elements $g_{k,k'}^{j}$ for the $j$th phonon mode in $MgB_2$ are defined by the transition amplitude between electronic states $|\vec{k}\rangle$ and $|\vec{k'}\rangle$ mediated by the total self-consistent change in the crystal potential $\delta V_{j}^{j'}$ induced by a frozen phonon displacement, expressed as $g_{k,k'}^{j} = \langle \vec{k} | \delta V_{j}^{j'} | \vec{k'} \rangle$.

extra.notations:
- $g_{k,k'}^{j}$: Linear electron-phonon matrix element for the $j$th phonon mode involving electronic states with wave vectors $\vec{k}$ and $\vec{k'}$.
- $\delta V_{j}^{j'}$: Induced self-consistent change in the crystal potential caused by a frozen phonon displacement corresponding to the $j$th phonon mode.
- $\vec{k}, \vec{k'}$: Wave vectors representing electronic states on the Fermi surface.
- $E(r,\theta)$: Total energy of the crystal as a function of the frozen phonon amplitude $r$ and the angular coordinate $\theta$.
- $E_0$: Equilibrium total energy of the crystal.
- $c_2, c_3, c_4, c_5$: Phenomenological fitting coefficients for the polynomial expansion of the total energy.
- $E_{2g}$: Doubly degenerate in-plane boron-boron stretching phonon modes in the $MgB_2$ crystal structure.
- $r, \theta$: Polar coordinates representing the displacement amplitude and direction, respectively, of the atoms in the plane for the $E_{2g}$ mode.

title: Assumption: Anharmonic potential energy model for E2g modes
content: In the $MgB_2$ crystal system, the variation of the total energy $E(r,\theta)$ with respect to the frozen phonon amplitude $r$ and angle $\theta$ for the degenerate in-plane boron-boron stretching modes ($E_{2g}$) is modeled using a fifth-order polynomial fit to account for phonon anharmonicity, given by the expression $E(r,\theta)=E_{0}+c_{2}r^{2}+c_{4}r^{4}+(c_{3}r^{3}+c_{5}r^{5})\cos(3\theta)$, where $E_0$ is the equilibrium energy and $c_n$ are the fitting constants.

title: Distribution of coupling strengths on the Fermi surface
content: The electron-phonon coupling strength $\lambda(\vec{k},\vec{k}^{\prime})$ varies by over an order of magnitude across the Fermi surface, exceeding 2.0 for scattering between states on the $\sigma$-bonding cylindrical sheets, while remaining weak ($0.1$ to $0.5$) for scattering involving the $\pi$-bonding sheets.

title: Definition of onset temperature
content: The superconducting onset temperature, denoted as the temperature at which the transition to superconductivity begins, is defined and estimated as the specific temperature at which the curvature in the electrical resistance versus temperature ($R$ vs. $T$) plot exhibits the most rapid change.

title: Anisotropic Eliashberg Equation
content:
The anisotropic Eliashberg equations for a superconductor at the transition temperature $T_c$ are defined by the following coupled equations for the renormalization function $Z(\vec{k},i\omega_{n})$ and the gap function $\Delta(\vec{k},i \omega_{n})$:
\[ Z(\vec{k},i\omega_{n})=1+f_{n}s_{n}\sum_{\vec{k}^{\prime}n^{\prime}}W_{\vec{k}^{\prime}}\lambda(\vec{k},\vec{k}^{\prime},n-n^{\prime})s_{n^{\prime}} \]
\[ Z(\vec{k},i\omega_{n})\Delta(\vec{k},i \omega_{n})=\sum_{\vec{k}^{\prime}n^{\prime}}W_{\vec{k}^{\prime}}f_{n^{\prime}}[\lambda(\vec{k},\vec{k}^{\prime},n-n^{\prime})-\mu^{*}(\omega_{c})]\Delta(\vec{k}^{\prime},i \omega_{n^{\prime}}) \]
where $\omega_{n}=(2n+1)\pi T_{c}$ are the Matsubara frequencies, $f_{n}=1/|2n+1|$, $s_{n} = \omega_{n}/|\omega_{n}|$, $W_{\vec{k}}$ is the fraction of the electronic density of states at wave vector $\vec{k}$ on the Fermi surface, $\lambda(\vec{k},\vec{k}^{\prime},n)$ is the electron-phonon interaction kernel, and $\mu^{*}(\omega_{c})$ is the dimensionless Coulomb pseudopotential evaluated at the cutoff frequency $\omega_{c}$.

- $T_{c}$: superconducting transition temperature
- $\vec{k}, \vec{k}^{\prime}$: electron wave vectors on the Fermi surface
- $i\omega_{n}$: Matsubara frequency, where $\omega_{n}=(2n+1)\pi T_{c}$ for an integer $n$
- $Z(\vec{k},i\omega_{n})$: anisotropic renormalization function
- $\Delta(\vec{k},i \omega_{n})$: anisotropic superconducting gap function
- $W_{\vec{k}}$: fraction of the electronic density of states at $\vec{k}$ on the Fermi surface
- $\lambda(\vec{k},\vec{k}^{\prime},n)$: electron-phonon interaction kernel between states $\vec{k}$ and $\vec{k}^{\prime}$
- $s_{n}$: sign of the Matsubara frequency, $s_{n} = \omega_{n}/|\omega_{n}|$
- $f_{n}$: frequency-dependent factor defined as $f_{n}=1/|2n+1|$
- $\mu^{*}(\omega_{c})$: dimensionless Coulomb pseudopotential at the cutoff frequency $\omega_{c}$
- $\epsilon_{F}$: Fermi energy
- $E(r,\theta)$: total energy of the in-plane boron-boron (B-B) stretching modes ($E_{2g}$)
- $r, \theta$: polar coordinates representing the amplitude and direction of the phonon distortion
- $c_{2}, c_{3}, c_{4}, c_{5}$: polynomial expansion coefficients for the potential energy surface

title: Coulomb Pseudopotential Definition
content:
The dimensionless Coulomb pseudopotential $\mu^{*}(\omega)$ at a frequency scale $\omega$ is defined by the relation:
\[ \mu^{*}(\omega)=\frac{\mu}{1+\mu\ln(\epsilon_{F}/\omega)} \]
where $\mu$ is the static Coulomb repulsion and $\epsilon_{F}$ is the Fermi energy of the system.

title: Anharmonic Potential Energy Surface
content:
For the in-plane boron-boron (B-B) stretching modes (denoted as $E_{2g}$ modes) in $MgB_{2}$, the total energy $E(r,\theta)$ associated with the phonon distortion is expressed using a polynomial expansion in polar coordinates $(r, \theta)$:
\[ E(r,\theta)=E_{0}+c_{2}r^{2}+c_{4}r^{4}+(c_{3}r^{3}+c_{5}r^{5})\cos(3\theta) \]
where $E_{0}$ is the equilibrium energy, $r$ is the radial displacement, $\theta$ is the angular coordinate, and $c_{2}, c_{3}, c_{4}, c_{5}$ are the expansion coefficients determined by fitting to first-principles total-energy calculations.

- $T_{c}$: superconducting transition temperature

title: Definition of Anharmonic Phonon Frequency
content: The harmonic phonon frequency of a vibrational mode is calculated classically using only the quadratic term $c_{2}r^{2}$ of the potential energy fit. In contrast, the anharmonic phonon frequency is defined as the energy difference between the two lowest quantum-mechanical vibrational states ($\Delta E = E_{1} - E_{0}$) calculated by solving the Schrödinger equation for the vibrational system using the full potential energy surface including the anharmonic terms (non-quadratic terms such as $r^3, r^4, r^5$ in the expansion).

title: Two-gap superconductivity prediction
content: The solution of the full anisotropic Eliashberg equation predicts a superconducting energy gap distribution that clusters into two distinct groups with values of approximately 2 meV and 7 meV.

title: Experimental Superconducting Transition Temperature
content: In the doped iron-based layered compound $\mathrm{LaFeAsO}_{1-x}\mathrm{F}_{x}$ containing $\mathrm{Fe}^{2+}$ square lattice sheets, the experimentally observed superconducting transition temperature is $T_{c} \geq 26$ K.

title: Ab Initio Calculation Methodology
content: The ab initio calculations of the electron-phonon spectral function $\alpha^{2}F(\omega)$ and the coupling constant $\lambda$ for the stoichiometric compound $\mathrm{LaFeAsO}$ are performed using linear response theory with ultrasoft pseudopotentials as implemented in the QUANTUM ESPRESSO package, utilizing a zone sampling of 1377 inequivalent $k$ points, a $4 \times 4 \times 2$ phonon mesh, and a plane wave energy cutoff of 50 Ry.

title: Definition of Logarithmically Averaged Frequency
content: In the context of Eliashberg theory for superconductivity, the quantity $\omega_{log}$ is defined such that its inverse, $\omega_{log}^{-1}$, represents the logarithmically averaged boson frequency of the system.

title: Insufficiency of Electron-Phonon Coupling
content: Based on ab initio calculations of the electron-phonon spectral function $\alpha^{2}F(\omega)$ and coupling $\lambda$ for stoichiometric LaFeAsO, the total coupling is determined to be $\lambda \sim 0.2$ with a logarithmically averaged boson frequency $\omega_{log} \sim 250$ K, which is insufficient to explain the experimental superconducting transition temperature $T_{c} \geq 26$ K.

title: Undoped Fermi Surface Structure
content: Based on density functional theory (DFT) calculations for stoichiometric, undoped $LaFeAsO$, the Fermi surface is characterized by a five-sheet topology consisting of two small electron cylinders centered around the tetragonal $M$ point of the Brillouin zone, two hole cylinders centered around the $\Gamma$ point, and a heavy three-dimensional (3D) hole pocket also centered around the $\Gamma$ point.

- $M$: The tetragonal Brillouin zone corner point $(\pi/a, \pi/a, 0)$, where $a$ is the lattice constant.
- WIEN: An implementation of the full-potential linearized augmented plane-wave method for electronic structure calculations.

- GGA: Generalized Gradient Approximation, a class of exchange-correlation functionals in DFT.

title: Magnetic indicators of superconductivity
content: In the Tl-Ca/Ba-Cu-O system, the presence of superconductivity is verified qualitatively by observing strong diamagnetic repulsion, characterized by the levitation of the sample over a magnetic field (Meissner effect), and is quantitatively characterized by measuring the temperature dependence of the alternating current (a.c.) magnetic susceptibility.

- $Tl_{2}O_{3}$: Thallium(III) oxide
- $CaO$: Calcium oxide
- $BaCu_{3}O_{4}$: Barium copper oxide precursor
- $T_{c}$: Superconducting transition temperature

title: Computational Methodology
content: The electronic structure of $LaFeAsO_{1-x}F_{x}$ is modeled using full-potential density functional theory (DFT) as implemented in the WIEN package; doping effects are treated via the virtual crystal approximation (VCA), and exchange-correlation effects are accounted for using the Perdew-Burke-Ernzerhof (PBE) generalized gradient approximation (GGA) functional.

title: Structural Inputs
content: The structural parameters used for density functional theory calculations of $LaFeAsO_{1-x}F_{x}$ include lattice parameters $a$ and $c$ obtained from experimental measurements, while the internal atomic coordinates (such as the $z$-positions of $La$ and $As$) are determined by minimizing the total energy within the calculation.

title: Fermi Surface Topology Evolution under Doping
content: Using full-potential DFT calculations with the virtual crystal approximation for doping $x=0.1$, the authors establish that the heavy 3D hole pocket around $\Gamma$ fills at electron doping levels of $x = 0.04-0.05$, simplifying the fermiology to a highly 2D electronic structure comprising two heavy hole cylinders and two lighter, larger electron cylinders.

content: Electronic structure calculations for the $LaFeAsO_{1-x}F_{x}$ system are performed using the WIEN full-potential calculation package within the virtual crystal approximation to model doping, employing the Perdew-Burke-Ernzerhof generalized gradient approximation (GGA) for the exchange-correlation functional.

content: The lattice parameters used for the density functional theory calculations of $LaFeAsO_{1-x}F_{x}$ are obtained from experimental structural data for the stoichiometric compound $LaFeAsO$.

title: Stoner Parameter for Iron
content: In the evaluation of the renormalized Pauli susceptibility for $Fe$-based layers, the Stoner parameter $I$ for Iron ($Fe$) is assumed to be $1.1$ eV.

title: Undoped Material Reference Properties
content: The undoped stoichiometric compound $LaFeAsO$ is characterized by proximity to a magnetic instability evidenced by a high magnetic susceptibility, and its Fermi surface, as calculated via density functional theory (DFT), comprises two small electron cylinders centered around the tetragonal $M$ point, two hole cylinders around the $\Gamma$ point, and a heavy three-dimensional (3D) hole pocket also located around the $\Gamma$ point.

title: Suppression of Ferromagnetic Instability via Doping
content: The authors determine that, in contrast to the undoped material, the doped compound ($x=0.1$) exhibits no ferromagnetic solution within Generalized Gradient Approximation (GGA) calculations; this suppression of magnetic instability is attributed to the filling of the heavy 3D hole pocket near $\Gamma$, which reduces the total density of states by a factor of 2.

title: General Condition for Spin-Fluctuation Mediated Pairing
content: Spin fluctuations (SFs) in a metallic system can induce either a triplet superconducting order parameter or a singlet superconducting order parameter that exhibits a sign change across different regions of the Fermi surface (FS); specifically, a repulsive interaction connecting well-separated FS pockets can support a strongly pairing state if the superconducting order parameters on the connected FS sheets have opposite signs.

title: Definition of Noninteracting Susceptibility
content: The noninteracting magnetic susceptibility $\chi_{0}(q,\omega)$ of an electronic system is defined as $\chi_{0}(q,\omega)=\sum_{k} \frac{f(\epsilon_{k})-f(\epsilon_{k+q})}{\epsilon_{k}-\epsilon_{k+q}-\omega-i\delta}$, where $f$ is the Fermi function, $\epsilon_{k}$ is the electronic band energy at wave vector $k$, $\omega$ is the frequency, and $\delta$ is a broadening parameter.

title: Discovery of High-Tc Superconductivity in the Tl–Ca/Ba–Cu–O System
content: Stable and reproducible bulk superconductivity is established in the Tl–Ca/Ba–Cu–O system, characterized by an onset temperature of 120 K (determined by the rapid change in curvature of the resistance-temperature plot) and zero resistance achieved above 100 K.

extra.notations:
- $Tl_{2}O_{3}$: Thallium(III) oxide
- $CaO$: Calcium oxide
- $BaCu_{3}O_{4}$: Barium copper oxide precursor
- $T_{c}$: Superconducting transition temperature

title: RPA Renormalized Susceptibility
content: Within the Random Phase Approximation (RPA), the renormalized magnetic susceptibility $\chi(q)$ is related to the noninteracting susceptibility $\chi_{0}(q)$ and the exchange interaction $J(q)$ by the expression $\chi(q) = \chi_{0}(q)/[1 - J(q)\chi_{0}(q)]$.

title: Relation of Susceptibility to Pairing Interaction
content: In spin-fluctuation mediated superconductors, the real part of the magnetic susceptibility, $\operatorname{Re}\chi$, governs the strength of the pairing interaction; for the singlet pairing channel, this interaction is proportional to $1/[1-J(q)\chi_{0}(q,0)]$, where $J(q)$ is the exchange interaction and $\chi_{0}(q,0)$ is the static noninteracting susceptibility.

title: Eliashberg Theory Limit for Conventional Superconductivity
content: In the framework of Eliashberg theory for conventional phonon-mediated superconductivity, the transition temperature $T_{c}$ is determined by the electron-phonon spectral function $\alpha^{2}F(\omega)$ and the coupling constant $\lambda$; for a system characterized by a total coupling $\lambda \approx 0.2$ and a logarithmically averaged boson frequency $\omega_{log} \approx 250$ K, the resulting $T_{c}$ is insufficient to account for observed values of $T_{c} \geq 26$ K.

title: Identification of Pairing-Mediating Spin Fluctuations
content: The authors identify the primary source of pairing interaction as nesting-related antiferromagnetic spin-density-wave-type fluctuations connecting the hole pockets around $\Gamma$ and the electron pockets around $M$, characterized by a broad peak in the calculated noninteracting susceptibility $\chi_{0}(q,\omega\to0)$ at the $M$ point (or $q = (\pi/\tilde{a}, 0)$ in the unfolded zone).

title: General rules for Spin Fluctuation pairing
content: In a superconducting system where the pairing is mediated by spin fluctuations (SFs), the resulting superconducting order parameter (OP) is restricted to being either a triplet state or a singlet state that undergoes a sign reversal ($s_{\pm}$ or $d$-wave type) across the Fermi surface.

title: Definition of non-interacting susceptibility
content: In the context of the electronic structure of $LaFeAsO_{1-x}F_{x}$, the non-interacting (Pauli) magnetic susceptibility $\chi_{0}$ as a function of wave vector $q$ and frequency $\omega$ is defined by the Lindhard-like expression $\chi_{0}(q,\omega)=\sum_{k} \frac{f(\epsilon_{k})-f(\epsilon_{k+q})}{\epsilon_{k}-\epsilon_{k+q}-\omega-i\delta}$, where $f(\epsilon)$ is the Fermi-Dirac distribution, $\epsilon_{k}$ is the electronic band energy, and $\delta$ is an infinitesimal positive real number.

title: Renormalized susceptibility relation
content: The renormalized magnetic susceptibility $\chi(q)$ for the $LaFeAsO_{1-x}F_{x}$ system is related to the non-interacting susceptibility $\chi_{0}(q)$ at zero frequency ($\omega \to 0$) through the Random Phase Approximation (RPA) form $\chi(q) = \chi_{0}(q) / [1 - J(q)\chi_{0}(q)]$, where $J(q)$ represents the wave-vector dependent exchange interaction comprising ferromagnetic and antiferromagnetic components.

title: Effective coupling in two-gap superconductors
content: For a multiband superconductor involving two sets of Fermi surface pockets (specifically hole pockets $h$ and electron pockets $e$), the superconducting transition temperature $T_{c}$ is determined by the maximal eigenvalue $\lambda_{eff}$ of the $2 \times 2$ coupling matrix $\lambda$, expressed as $\lambda_{eff} = \frac{1}{2} \left[ \lambda_{hh} + \lambda_{ee} + \sqrt{4\lambda_{eh}\lambda_{he} + (\lambda_{ee} - \lambda_{hh})^2} \right]$, where $\lambda_{ij}$ are the inter-pocket and intra-pocket coupling constants.

title: Relation between phonons and spin fluctuations in coupling
content: In the $LaFeAsO_{1-x}F_{x}$ system, the total diagonal coupling constants $\lambda_{ii}$ (where $i \in \{h, e\}$ refers to hole or electron pockets) are determined by the competition between different boson exchange mechanisms, given by $\lambda_{ii} = \lambda_{ii}^{ph} - \lambda_{ii}^{sf}$, where $\lambda_{ii}^{ph} > 0$ denotes the attractive phonon-mediated contribution and $\lambda_{ii}^{sf} > 0$ denotes the repulsive spin-fluctuation-mediated contribution.

title: Assumption: Validity of DFT and Linear Response Theory
content: The electronic band structure, Fermi surface geometry, and electron-phonon coupling constants $\lambda^{ph}$ of the stoichiometric and doped $LaFeAsO$ compounds are accurately modeled using Density Functional Theory (DFT) within the Perdew-Burke-Ernzerhof (PBE) generalized gradient approximation (GGA) and density functional linear response theory employing ultrasoft pseudopotentials.

title: Melting point of BaCu3O4
content: The compound $BaCu_{3}O_{4}$ possesses the lowest melting point among all barium-copper oxide (Ba-Cu-O) phases.

title: Assumption: Virtual Crystal Approximation
content: The effects of fluorine (F) doping on the electronic structure of $LaFeAsO_{1-x}F_{x}$, specifically the shift in the Fermi level and the resulting changes in the topology of the electron and hole Fermi surface cylinders, are modeled using the virtual crystal approximation (VCA).

title: Proposal of the s± Superconducting State
content: The authors propose that the superconducting state in doped LaFeAsO is an unconventional extended $s$-wave state, denoted as $s_{\pm}$, characterized by a full gap with a sign reversal (discontinuous $\pi$ phase shift) of the order parameter between the hole-pocket and electron-pocket Fermi surface sheets.

title: Definition of the s± State
content: The $s_{\pm}$ superconducting state is defined as an extended s-wave pairing state characterized by a sign reversal of the phase of the superconducting order parameter (OP) between different sets of Fermi surface sheets, specifically exhibiting opposite signs between the hole-like cylinders located around the $\Gamma$ point and the electron-like cylinders located around the M point of the Brillouin zone.

title: Coherence Factors with Sign Reversal
content: In a multi-band superconducting state where the order parameter changes sign between different Fermi surface sheets, the coherence factors governing the transition matrix elements for exciting Bogliubov quasiparticles between sheets with opposite signs of the order parameter are reversed in sign relative to the coherence factors in a conventional superconductor where the order parameter sign is uniform.

title: NMR Relaxation Rate Averaging
content: The nuclear magnetic resonance (NMR) relaxation rate $1/T_{1}$ is proportional to the summation or average of the imaginary part of the dynamic spin susceptibility contributions over all possible wave vectors $\mathbf{q}$ within the first Brillouin zone.

title: Dominance of AFM Spin Fluctuations
content: In the doped iron-based superconductor LaFeAsO, the spin fluctuation spectrum is dominated by antiferromagnetic (AFM) spin fluctuations at wave vectors $\mathbf{q}$ near the nesting-related vectors connecting hole and electron Fermi surface pockets, specifically $\mathbf{q} \approx (\pi/a, \pi/a)$ in the Brillouin zone corresponding to the two-Fe unit cell.

title: Predicted Experimental Signatures of the s± State
content: For the proposed $s_{\pm}$ state, the authors derive specific experimental signatures: the spin susceptibility at $q=0$ is predicted to decay exponentially below $T_{c}$ without a coherence peak, whereas the susceptibility for $q \approx (\pi/a, \pi/a)$ will exhibit a coherence peak detectable by neutron scattering; consequently, the coherence peak in the NMR relaxation rate is expected to be strongly reduced or absent.

title: Lattice Structure of LaFeAsO
content: The compound LaFeAsO possesses a tetragonal layered crystal structure where Iron (Fe) atoms form a square lattice. Each Fe layer is positioned between layers of Lanthanum and Oxygen (LaO). The structural parameters for this configuration are defined by the lattice constants $a = 4.03552 \AA$ and $c = 8.7393 \AA$, and internal atomic coordinates $z_{La} = 0.1415$ and $z_{As} = 0.6512$.

title: First-Principles Calculation Method
content: The electronic band structure of LaFeAsO is determined via density functional theory using the Quantum-ESPRESSO software package. The calculation employs the exchange-correlation functional of J. P. Perdew, K. Burke, and M. Ernzerhof [Phys. Rev. B 54, 16533 (1996)]. The electronic wave functions are expanded using a plane-wave basis set with a kinetic energy cutoff of 40 Ry. Integration over the Brillouin zone is performed using $10 \times 10 \times 10$ k-point meshes generated by the special points technique of H. J. Monkhorst and J. D. Pack [Phys. Rev. B 13, 5188 (1976)].

title: Maximally Localized Wannier Functions Method
content: Maximally localized Wannier functions for the Fe $3d$ states in LaFeAsO are constructed within an energy window of $-2\text{ eV} < \epsilon_{k} - E_{F} < 2.6\text{ eV}$, where $\epsilon_{k}$ is the Bloch state eigenenergy and $E_{F}$ is the Fermi energy. These functions are centered at the two Fe sites within the original unit cell and are categorized into five orbital symmetries: orbital 1 ($d_{3Z^{2}-R^{2}}$), orbital 2 ($d_{XZ}$), orbital 3 ($d_{YZ}$), orbital 4 ($d_{X^{2}-Y^{2}}$), and orbital 5 ($d_{XY}$), where the coordinates $X, Y, Z$ correspond to the axes of the original tetragonal unit cell.

title: Assumption: Brillouin Zone Unfolding Principle
content: Based on the equivalence of the two Iron (Fe) atoms within the tetragonal unit cell of LaFeAsO—where each Fe atom shares an identical local atomic environment—the electronic system can be mapped from a two-Fe-atom unit cell to an effective single-Fe-atom unit cell. This mapping is achieved by unfolding the Brillouin zone, resulting in a five-band model defined on a square lattice. In this unfolded representation, the $x$ and $y$ axes are rotated by $45^{\circ}$ relative to the $X$ and $Y$ axes of the original tetragonal unit cell.

title: Construction of a minimal five-band tight-binding model for LaFeAsO
content:
The authors derive a microscopic electronic model for LaFeAsO, concluding that a minimal description requires the inclusion of all five Fe $3d$ bands due to strong hybridization and heavy entanglement arising from the tetrahedral coordination of As atoms. By unfolding the Brillouin zone to an effective square lattice, they establish a two-dimensional tight-binding Hamiltonian:
$H_{0}=\sum_{ij}\sum_{\mu
u}\sum_{\sigma}[t(x_{i}-x_{j},y_{i}-y_{j};\mu,
u)c_{i\mu\sigma}^{\dagger}c_{j
u\sigma}+ 	ext{H.c.}]+\sum_{i\mu\sigma}\varepsilon_{\mu}n_{i\mu\sigma}$
where the orbital indices $\mu,
u$ run from 1 to 5 ($d_{3Z^{2}-R^{2}}, d_{XZ}, d_{YZ}, d_{X^{2}-Y^{2}}, d_{XY}$). Specific on-site energies are determined as $(\varepsilon_{1}, \varepsilon_{2}, \varepsilon_{3}, \varepsilon_{4}, \varepsilon_{5}) = (10.75, 10.96, 10.96, 11.12, 10.62)$ eV, and specific hopping integrals $t(\Delta x, \Delta y; \mu, 
u)$ are calculated and tabulated (Table I) to reproduce the first-principles band structure.

title: Orbital symmetries
content: The basis set for the electronic model of LaFeAsO consists of five maximally localized Wannier functions centered at the iron (Fe) sites, which correspond to the five atomic $d$-orbital symmetries: $d_{3Z^{2}-R^{2}}$ (orbital 1), $d_{XZ}$ (orbital 2), $d_{YZ}$ (orbital 3), $d_{X^{2}-Y^{2}}$ (orbital 4), and $d_{XY}$ (orbital 5).

content: A minimal electronic model for the iron-based superconductor LaFeAsO requires the inclusion of all five Fe $3d$ bands because the bands are heavily entangled and the orbitals exhibit strong hybridization due to the tetrahedral coordination of Arsenic (As) atoms around Iron (Fe).

title: Identification of extended s-wave pairing driven by disconnected Fermi surface nesting
content: Based on a random-phase approximation (RPA) analysis of the five-band model, the authors conclude that the superconducting pairing symmetry is an extended $s$-wave state. This state is characterized by a sign change of the gap function between the hole pockets ($\alpha_{1}, \alpha_{2}$ centered at $k=(0,0)$) and the electron pockets ($\beta_{1}, \beta_{2}$ centered at $(\pi, 0)$ and $(0, \pi)$). The pairing mechanism is attributed to multiple spin-fluctuation modes, specifically dominated by the nesting vector $\sim(\pi, 0)$ across the disconnected $\alpha$ and $\beta$ Fermi surfaces, which enhances the spin susceptibility peaks at $(\pi, 0)$ and $(0, \pi)$.

title: Five-band Tight-binding Hamiltonian
content:
In a square lattice model for $LaFeAsO_{1-x}F_{x}$ using an unfolded Brillouin zone, the non-interacting in-plane tight-binding Hamiltonian $H_{0}$ is defined as
\[ H_{0}=\sum_{ij}\sum_{\mu
u}\sum_{\sigma}[t(x_{i}-x_{j},y_{i}-y_{j};\mu,
u)c_{i\mu\sigma}^{\dagger}c_{j
u\sigma}+t(x_{j}-x_{i},y_{j}-y_{i};
u,\mu)c_{j
u\sigma}^{\dagger}c_{i\mu\sigma}]+\sum_{i\mu\sigma}\varepsilon_{\mu}n_{i\mu\sigma}, \]
where $c_{i\mu\sigma}^{\dagger}$ creates an electron with spin $\sigma$ on the $\mu$-th Wannier orbital at site $i$, $n_{i\mu\sigma} = c_{i\mu\sigma}^{\dagger} c_{i\mu\sigma}$ is the corresponding number operator, and the indices $\mu, 
u \in \{1, 2, 3, 4, 5\}$ label the five $Fe$ $3d$ orbitals ($d_{3Z^{2}-R^{2}}$, $d_{XZ}$, $d_{YZ}$, $d_{X^{2}-Y^{2}}$, $d_{XY}$). The parameters $t(x_{i}-x_{j}, y_{i}-y_{j}; \mu, 
u)$ represent the hopping integrals and $\varepsilon_{\mu}$ denote the on-site energies, both derived from the electronic band structure of the material.

extra.notations:
- $H_{0}$: Non-interacting tight-binding Hamiltonian.
- $c_{i\mu\sigma}^{\dagger}$ ($c_{i\mu\sigma}$): Creation (annihilation) operator for an electron with spin $\sigma$ on the $\mu$-th orbital at site $i$.
- $n_{i\mu\sigma}$: Number operator for an electron with spin $\sigma$ on the $\mu$-th orbital at site $i$, defined as $c_{i\mu\sigma}^{\dagger} c_{i\mu\sigma}$.
- $t(x_{i}-x_{j}, y_{i}-y_{j}; \mu, \nu)$: Hopping integral between orbital $\mu$ at site $i$ and orbital $\nu$ at site $j$.
- $\varepsilon_{\mu}$: On-site energy of the $\mu$-th Wannier orbital.
- $U$: Intraorbital Coulomb interaction strength.
- $U'$: Interorbital Coulomb interaction strength.
- $J$: Hund's coupling strength.
- $J'$: Pair-hopping interaction strength.
- RPA: Random Phase Approximation.
- $\hat{\phi}$: Superconducting gap function matrix.
- $\lambda$: Eigenvalue of the linearized Eliashberg equation.

title: RPA and Eliashberg Equation Method
content: In a multiorbital electronic system, the superconducting gap function $\hat{\phi}$ (represented as a $5 \times 5$ matrix in orbital space) and the associated eigenvalue $\lambda$ are determined by solving the linearized Eliashberg equation, into which the Green's function and the effective pairing interactions are substituted; these interactions are derived from the spin and orbital susceptibilities calculated within the multiorbital random-phase approximation (RPA).

title: Nesting and Spin Fluctuation Principle
content: In the context of spin-fluctuation-mediated superconductivity, strong nesting between different sections of the Fermi surface (characterized by nesting vectors $\mathbf{q}$) enhances the magnetic susceptibility at those vectors, and the resulting spin fluctuations favor a sign change in the superconducting gap function $\Delta(\mathbf{k})$ between the Fermi surface pockets connected by $\mathbf{k}' = \mathbf{k} + \mathbf{q}$.

title: Definition of superconducting onset temperature
content: The onset temperature of a superconducting transition is defined as the temperature at which the rate of change of the curvature (the second derivative) in the electrical resistance ($R$) versus temperature ($T$) plot, $d^{2}R/dT^{2}$, is maximal.

title: Gap Magnitude Definition
content: In a multiorbital superconducting system where the gap function $\hat{\phi}$ is a matrix in the orbital representation, the magnitude of the gap for a specific band $i$, accounting for interband effects and band entanglement, is characterized by the scalar quantity $\sqrt{(\hat{\phi}\hat{\phi}^{\dagger})_{ii}}$, where $ii$ denotes the diagonal element of the product of the gap matrix $\hat{\phi}$ and its adjoint $\hat{\phi}^{\dagger}$ corresponding to band $i$.

title: Existence of nodal lines on electron pockets due to competing spin fluctuations
content: The authors find that for the specific parameter set ($U=1.2, U'=0.9, J=J'=0.15$ eV), the $s$-wave gap function possesses nodal lines that intersect the $\beta$ (electron) Fermi surfaces. This nodal structure arises because spin fluctuations due to the $\beta_{1}-\beta_{2}$ nesting (vectors $\sim(\pi, \pi/2)$ and $(\pi/2, \pi)$) favor a sign change between the $\beta_{1}$ and $\beta_{2}$ pockets, competing with the dominant $\alpha-\beta$ interaction. Additionally, the authors determine that off-diagonal (interband) elements of the gap matrix are non-negligible, resulting in a finite gap magnitude $\sqrt{(\hat{\phi}\hat{\phi}^{\dagger})_{44}}$ over the entire Brillouin zone, although a minimum (valley) remains where the diagonal nodal lines intersect the Fermi surface.

title: Tight-Binding Hamiltonian
content:
For an effective five-band model on a square lattice representing the $LaFeAsO_{1-x}F_{x}$ system, the in-plane tight-binding Hamiltonian $H_{0}$ is defined as $H_{0}=\sum_{ij}\sum_{\mu
u}\sum_{\sigma}[t(x_{i}-x_{j},y_{i}-y_{j};\mu,
u)c_{i\mu\sigma}^{\dagger}c_{j
u\sigma}+t(x_{j}-x_{i},y_{j}-y_{i};
u,\mu)c_{j
u\sigma}^{\dagger}c_{i\mu\sigma}]+\sum_{i\mu\sigma}\varepsilon_{\mu}n_{i\mu\sigma}$, where $c_{i\mu\sigma}^{\dagger}$ is the creation operator for an electron with spin $\sigma$ on the $\mu$-th orbital at site $i$, $n_{i\mu\sigma} = c_{i\mu\sigma}^{\dagger} c_{i\mu\sigma}$ is the number operator, $\varepsilon_{\mu}$ are the orbital-dependent on-site energies, and $t(\Delta x, \Delta y; \mu, 
u)$ are the hopping integrals between orbitals $\mu$ and $
u$ at spatial displacement $(x_{i}-x_{j}, y_{i}-y_{j})$.

title: Linearized Eliashberg Equation and Gap Function
content: The superconducting transition temperature $T_{c}$ and the symmetry of the superconducting gap are determined by solving the linearized Eliashberg equation, wherein the Green function and effective pairing interactions (derived from spin and orbital susceptibilities) are utilized to calculate the gap function matrix and the eigenvalue $\lambda$; $T_{c}$ is defined as the temperature at which the largest eigenvalue $\lambda$ reaches unity.

title: Nesting Vectors and Fermi Surface Topology
content: In the unfolded Brillouin zone for the $LaFeAsO_{1-x}F_{x}$ model at a band filling of $n=6.1$, the Fermi surface consists of two concentric hole pockets ($\alpha_{1}, \alpha_{2}$) centered at $(0,0)$ and two electron pockets ($\beta_{1}, \beta_{2}$) centered at $(\pi, 0)$ and $(0, \pi)$, respectively, which are connected by two primary types of nesting vectors: vectors of approximately $(\pi, 0)$ and $(\pi, \pi)$ connecting $\alpha$ and $\beta$ pockets, and vectors of approximately $(\pi, \pi/2)$ and $(\pi/2, \pi)$ connecting the $\beta_{1}$ and $\beta_{2}$ electron pockets.

title: Mechanism of Gap Sign Change via Spin Fluctuations
content: In a multiorbital system where superconducting pairing is mediated by spin fluctuations, the fluctuation modes associated with specific Fermi surface nesting vectors favor a sign change in the superconducting gap function $\phi(\mathbf{k})$ between the Fermi surface sheets connected by those vectors; specifically, spin fluctuations arising from nesting between electron pockets $\beta_{1}$ and $\beta_{2}$ promote a sign change in the gap between the $\beta_{1}$ and $\beta_{2}$ surfaces.

title: Effect of Electron Correlation on Band Structure
content: Dynamical mean-field theory calculations indicate that electron correlation effects in $LaFeAsO$ increase the crystal field splitting of the $Fe$ $d$-bands, a modification that transforms the metallic state predicted by local-density approximations into a band-semiconducting state.

title: Definition of d-wave Pairing Symmetry
content: The $d_{x^{2}-y^{2}}$-wave pairing symmetry (corresponding to $d_{XY}$ symmetry in the original, folded Brillouin zone) is defined as a superconducting state in which the gap function $\phi(\mathbf{k})$ exhibits a sign change between the $\beta_{1}$ electron pocket (centered at $(\pi, 0)$) and the $\beta_{2}$ electron pocket (centered at $(0, \pi)$).

title: d-wave pairing as a competitive alternative candidate
content: The authors demonstrate that if the $\alpha$ (hole) Fermi surfaces are absent or ineffective (simulated by artificially shifting crystal field splitting or adjusting filling to $n=6.3$), the system realizes a $d_{x^{2}-y^{2}}$-wave pairing symmetry (gap changing sign between $\beta_{1}$ and $\beta_{2}$). Consequently, the authors conclude that $d$-wave pairing remains a valid candidate for the material depending on the precise effects of electron correlation and band filling on the Fermi surface topology.

title: Effect of Calcium substitution in Y- and La-based superconductors
content: The substitution of 50% of barium atoms with calcium atoms in the superconducting compound $YBa_{2}Cu_{3}O_{7-x}$ results in a depression of the superconducting transition temperature, whereas an equivalent 50% substitution of calcium for barium in the compound $LaBa_{2}Cu_{3}O_{7-x}$ results in an increase in the superconducting transition temperature.

title: Specific Heat Evidence for Bulk Superconductivity
content: The presence of a specific heat discontinuity or anomaly (a jump in $C_{p}$) at the superconducting transition temperature $T_{c}$ serves as experimental confirmation that the superconducting state is a bulk property of the entire material volume rather than a filamentary or surface effect.

title: Discovery of superconductivity in electron-doped BaFe1.8Co0.2As2
content: The authors report the synthesis of single crystals of $BaFe_{1.8}Co_{0.2}As_{2}$ which exhibit bulk superconductivity with an onset transition temperature $T_{c}^{onset} = 22$ K. This result constitutes the first demonstration of superconductivity induced by electron doping (substitution of Co on the Fe site) in the $BaFe_{2}As_{2}$ family of materials, contrasting with previous hole-doped analogues, and demonstrates that the system tolerates considerable disorder in the FeAs planes.

title: Superconducting Transition Properties of Tl2Ca1.5BaCu3O8.5+x
content: A furnace-cooled sample with the nominal composition $Tl_{2}Ca_{1.5}BaCu_{3}O_{8.5+x}$ exhibits a superconducting transition midpoint at 112 K and a zero-resistance temperature at 103 K, which are the highest values recorded among the prepared Tl–Ca/Ba–Cu–O samples.

title: Crystal structure and lattice parameters
content: X-ray diffraction analysis determines that the Co-doped compound $BaFe_{1.8}Co_{0.2}As_{2}$ crystallizes in the tetragonal $ThCr_{2}Si_{2}$ structure (space group I4/mmm) with refined lattice constants $a = 3.9639(4)$ Å and $c = 12.980(1)$ Å. Compared to the parent compound $BaFe_{2}As_{2}$, Co doping results in a 0.26% decrease in the $c$-axis length while the $a$-axis remains unchanged within experimental uncertainty.

title: Relationship between Hall coefficient and carrier type
content: In the characterization of charge transport in a conductor or semiconductor, the sign of the Hall coefficient $R_{H}$ serves as an indicator of the dominant charge carrier type, such that a negative $R_{H}$ corresponds to a system where electrons are the primary contributors to the Hall voltage.

title: Single-band carrier concentration formula
content: For a material system approximated by a single-band conduction model, the carrier concentration $n$ is defined by the relationship $n = \frac{1}{q R_{H}}$, where $R_{H}$ is the Hall coefficient and $q$ represents the elementary charge of the carrier.

title: Assumption: Indicator of magnetic contribution to Hall voltage
content: In the analysis of the Hall effect, a linear dependence of the Hall resistivity $\rho_{H}$ on the applied magnetic field $H$ is taken to indicate that there are no significant anomalous or magnetic contributions to the measured Hall voltage.

title: Assumption: Upper bound interpretation in two-band model
content: In a physical system characterized by a two-band model (containing both electron and hole bands at the Fermi level), the carrier concentration $n$ calculated using the single-band formula $n = \frac{1}{q R_{H}}$ provides a mathematical upper bound on the actual concentration of electrons rather than an exact density.

title: Characterization of charge carriers via Hall effect
content: Hall-effect measurements on $BaFe_{1.8}Co_{0.2}As_{2}$ reveal a negative Hall coefficient $R_{H}$ between 30 and 300 K, indicating that electrons are the dominant charge carriers. The carrier concentration $n$, estimated using a single-band approximation $n = 1/(qR_{H})$, is found to be approximately $6 \times 10^{21}\ \text{cm}^{-3}$ at 300 K, confirming that Co substitution effectively electron-dopes the FeAs layers.

content: In a type-II superconductor, the lower critical field $H_{c1}$ is defined as the magnetic field strength at which the magnetization $M(H)$ first deviates from the linear Meissner behavior characterized by perfect diamagnetism.

title: Lattice contraction upon F-doping
content: The LaOFeAs phase crystallizes in the tetragonal P4/nmm space group, and the lattice constants decrease systematically with $F^{-}$ substitution: the undoped samples have lattice constants $a = 0.403552(8)$ nm and $c = 0.87393(2)$ nm, whereas the 5 atom % F-doped samples have $a = 0.40320(1)$ nm and $c = 0.87263(3)$ nm.

extra.notations:
- $LaOMPn$: A class of quaternary oxypnictides where $La$ is Lanthanum, $O$ is Oxygen, $M$ is a transition metal (Mn, Fe, Co, or Ni), and $Pn$ is a pnictogen (P or As).
- $P4/nmm$: A specific tetragonal space group symmetry.
- $FeAs$: Iron arsenide, which can occur as an impurity phase in the synthesis of iron-based oxypnictides.
- Vegard's volume rule: An empirical heuristic stating that a linear relation exists, at constant temperature, between the crystal lattice parameter of an alloy and the concentrations of the constituent elements.
- $x$: The molar fraction of a dopant (specifically $F^{-}$ substituting for $O^{2-}$ in $La[O_{1-x}F_{x}]FeAs$).

title: Indicator of Bulk Superconductivity
content: The presence of strong diamagnetic repulsion in a material is a physical property that serves as an experimental indicator of the existence of bulk superconductivity within that material.

title: Type-II superconductivity characteristics
content: A hallmark of type-II superconductivity is that the resistive transition from the normal to the superconducting state shifts to lower temperatures and exhibits an increase in transition width (broadening) upon the application of an increasing external magnetic field.

title: Full magnetic shielding condition
content: For a superconducting sample, a zero-field-cooled ($zfc$) effective magnetic susceptibility $4\pi\chi_{eff}$ that reaches a value of $-1$ (often referred to as saturating to 1 in magnitude) indicates that the sample volume is fully shielded from the external magnetic field due to the Meissner effect.

title: Demagnetization correction
content: The effective magnetic susceptibility $\chi_{eff}$, which accounts for the internal field of a sample, is related to the experimentally measured susceptibility $\chi$ and the sample-dependent demagnetization factor $N$ by the geometric correction formula $\chi_{eff} = \chi / (1 - N\chi)$.

title: Superconducting critical parameters and bulk nature
content: Magnetization and specific heat measurements confirm the bulk nature of the superconductivity, with a specific heat anomaly peaking at 20 K. The material is a type-II superconductor with a lower critical field $H_{c1} \approx 200$ Oe at 1.8 K, an upper critical field $H_{c2}$ exceeding 7 T, and a critical current density $J_{c} \approx 1.8 \times 10^{4}\ \text{A cm}^{-2}$ along the ab plane derived using the Bean critical state model.

title: LDA Calculation Methodology
content: Density functional calculations for Co-doped $BaFe_{2}As_{2}$ are performed within the local density approximation (LDA) using supercells with $Ba_{4}Fe_{7}CoAs_{8}$ compositions, where the lattice parameters (a and c) are held fixed at the experimental values while all internal atomic coordinates are relaxed to their minimum energy configurations.

- $E_{F}$: Fermi energy
- $q$: Wave vector transfer (momentum transfer)
- $T_{c}$: Superconducting transition temperature

title: Electronic Structure of Parent Material
content: The Fe-based parent materials, such as $BaFe_{2}As_{2}$, are low carrier density metals characterized by an electronic structure featuring heavy hole Fermi surfaces located at the Brillouin zone center and lighter electron Fermi surface sections located around the Brillouin zone corner.

title: Effect of Intraband Scattering
content: In a superconducting system, intraband scattering processes (scattering within a single Fermi surface) act to average the superconducting order parameter across the momentum states of that specific Fermi surface.

extra.notations:
- LDA: Local Density Approximation
- DOS: Density of States
- $E_{F}$: Fermi energy
- $q$: Wave vector transfer (momentum transfer)
- $T_{c}$: Superconducting transition temperature

title: Destruction of Nodal States by Scattering
content: Superconducting order parameters that exhibit nodes or sign changes on a given Fermi surface, such as an axial p-wave state, are suppressed or destroyed by the averaging effect of intraband scattering, whereas s-like states (which do not change sign on a given set of hole or electron Fermi surfaces) are robust against such scattering.

title: Analogy to MgB2
content: The behavior of defects and their influence on two-band superconductivity in Fe-based superconductors, specifically regarding the separation of scattering effects between different bands, follows the physical analogy of $MgB_{2}$, where interband scattering is relatively weak compared to intraband scattering.

title: Manifestation of Diamagnetism
content: The stable levitation of a material sample over a magnetic field (such as a field of approximately $5,000$ G) is a qualitative physical manifestation of strong diamagnetic repulsion, which is associated with the Meissner effect in superconductors.

title: Theoretical implications for scattering and pairing symmetry
content: Local Density Approximation (LDA) supercell calculations for $BaFe_{1.8}Co_{0.2}As_{2}$ indicate that Co dopants strongly affect the hole section states but produce relatively weak interband scattering. Based on the robustness of superconductivity against this in-plane disorder and the weak interband scattering, the authors conclude that the superconducting state is likely an s-wave state (potentially a generalized s-state with a sign change between hole and electron surfaces) rather than a nodal state which would be destroyed by the disorder.

title: Contact issues with hydrogen gas loading
content: In the synthesis of lanthanum superhydrides from a mixture of elemental lanthanum ($La$) and hydrogen gas ($H_{2}$) loaded into a diamond anvil cell gasket assembly, the process frequently fails to establish or maintain stable physical contact between the resulting synthesized superhydride material and the electrical electrodes required for transport measurements.

content: The complete thermal dehydrogenation of one mole of ammonia borane ($NH_{3}BH_{3}$) yields three moles of hydrogen gas ($H_{2}$) and one mole of cubic boron nitride ($cBN$).

content: Cubic boron nitride ($cBN$) is an electrical insulator that, when present in a high-pressure gasket assembly, acts as a solid pressure-transmitting medium and a structural support that holds a sample securely against electrical contacts.

content: The synthesis of the cubic phase of lanthanum superhydride, denoted as $LaH_{10\pm x}$ (where $x$ is between -1 and +2), from elemental lanthanum and hydrogen requires a high-pressure environment exceeding 175 GPa and a temperature range of 1000 K to 1500 K.

title: Temperature constraint for phase purity
content: During the synthesis of cubic lanthanum superhydride ($LaH_{10\pm x}$) at high pressures, the sample temperature must be restricted to below 1800 K to ensure the completion of the phase transformation while preventing the formation of undesired additional chemical or structural phases.

title: Synthesis method using ammonia borane for electrical contact preservation
content: The authors developed a high-pressure synthesis technique for superhydrides that utilizes ammonia borane ($NH_{3}BH_{3}$) as a hydrogen source and solid pressure medium. This method, combined with modulated pulsed laser heating (typically 300-ms pulses and 30-$\mu$m spot size) at pressures of 180–200 GPa, enables the synthesis of lanthanum superhydride while maintaining the physical integrity of sample-probe contacts for four-probe electrical transport measurements, overcoming the electrode damage issues common with pure $H_2$ loading.

content: Under conditions of complete dehydrogenation, one mole of ammonia borane ($NH_3BH_3$, abbreviated as AB) undergoes a chemical decomposition to yield three moles of molecular hydrogen ($H_2$) and a solid residue of insulating cubic boron nitride (cBN).

title: Structure and Stoichiometry of Lanthanum Superhydride
content: In the synthesis of lanthanum superhydride from elemental lanthanum (La) and molecular hydrogen ($H_2$) at a pressure of 170 GPa and temperatures of approximately 1000 K, the lanthanum atoms arrange into a face-centered cubic (fcc) lattice. This synthesized structure exhibits a compressibility consistent with theoretical predictions for the cubic metallic phase of $LaH_{10}$, and based on experimental and theoretical constraints, the material possesses a stoichiometry of $LaH_{10\pm x}$, where the parameter $x$ ranges between -1 and +2.

title: Synthesis and structure of LaH10±x from ammonia borane
content: In situ X-ray diffraction confirms that the reaction of La and ammonia borane at pressures above 175 GPa and temperatures of 1000–1500 K yields a face-centered cubic (fcc) lattice structure of $LaH_{10\pm x}$. This structure is consistent with the superhydride phase previously synthesized from La and pure $H_2$, verifying that the ammonia borane route produces the targeted cubic metallic phase.

content: The technique of Norton is an experimental method used to determine the temperature dependence of the alternating current (a.c.) susceptibility of a material sample to identify its superconducting transition characteristics.

title: Theoretical prediction of LaH10 properties
content: Theoretical structure searches using density functional theory and systematic global optimization in the lanthanum-hydrogen (La—H) system predict that the $\text{LaH}_{10}$ phase possesses a cubic clathrate-type structure in which each lanthanum (La) atom is surrounded by a cage of 32 hydrogen (H) atoms, characterized by H—H interatomic distances of approximately $1.1$ Å. Under a hydrostatic pressure of $210$ GPa, this phase is predicted to be a high-temperature superconductor with a critical temperature ($T_{c}$) of approximately $270$ K.

title: Structural characteristics of lanthanum superhydride
content: In situ synchrotron X-ray diffraction measurements of lanthanum superhydride synthesized at a pressure of $170$ GPa and temperatures of approximately $1000$ K demonstrate that the lanthanum (La) atoms are arranged in a face-centered cubic (fcc) lattice. The measured bulk modulus and compressibility of this synthesized phase are consistent with the theoretical values calculated for the predicted cubic metallic $\text{LaH}_{10}$ phase.

title: Observation of resistance drop at 260 K
content: Four-probe electrical resistance measurements on the synthesized $LaH_{10\pm x}$ samples at pressures between 180 and 200 GPa reveal a significant drop in resistance starting at approximately 260 K on cooling. Specifically, in one sample (Sample A, initial pressure 188 GPa), the resistance dropped abruptly by a factor of $>10^{3}$ at 260 K and remained constant down to 150 K, with the transition being reversible upon warming (shifting to 245 K).

title: Current dependence of the transition temperature
content: The transition temperature observed in the electrical resistance measurements decreases with increasing applied current. In Sample F, the transition temperature decreased by 10 K when the current was increased from 0.1 mA to 1 mA, and appeared to decrease further at higher currents, behavior that the authors identify as characteristic of the superconducting phase transition.

title: Verification of Bulk Superconductivity via Magnetic Response
content: The Tl–Ca/Ba–Cu–O samples display strong diamagnetic repulsion indicative of bulk superconductivity, demonstrated by the levitation of a sample with nominal composition $Tl_{2}Ca_{2}BaCu_{3}O_{9+x}$ over a $\sim 5,000$ G magnetic field and confirmed by AC susceptibility measurements.

title: Alternative explanation for resistivity change
content: An iso-structural electronic transition induced by temperature, characterized by a significant increase in electrical conductivity in the low-temperature phase while maintaining the same crystal structure, serves as a possible alternative explanation for an observed resistivity change in a metallic system, representing a metal-to-metal transition rather than a superconducting transition.

title: Metallic nature of the high-temperature phase
content: Optical observations of lanthanum superhydride ($LaH_{10}$) at pressures near 170-200 GPa indicate that the high-temperature phase, corresponding to the normal state above the observed resistance drop, exhibits metallic behavior.

title: Absence of structural transition at the critical temperature
content: Low-temperature X-ray diffraction measurements performed across the temperature range of the resistance drop reveal no change in volume and no indication of a structural phase transition. This result supports the conclusion that the observed resistance drop at $\sim260$ K is electronic in origin (superconductivity) rather than a result of a temperature-induced iso-structural metal-metal transition.

content: Applied hydrostatic pressure enhances the superconducting transition temperature $T_{c}$ of the La-Ba-Cu-O compound system at a rate greater than $10^{-3}$ K bar$^{-1}$, raising the onset $T_{c}$ to 57 K and enabling the achievement of a zero-resistance state at 40 K.

title: Inverse relationship between phase purity and onset Tc
content: In copper-oxide based superconducting compounds, reducing the transition width by increasing the phase purity toward a pure $K_{2}NiF_{4}$ structure results in a reduction of the onset superconducting transition temperature $T_{c}$, while the temperature of the main transition near 37 K remains constant.

title: Superconductivity of Tl-Ba-Cu-O
content: Samples in the Tl-Ba-Cu-O chemical system exhibit bulk superconductivity characterized by zero electrical resistance at temperatures just above the boiling point of liquid nitrogen ($77$ K).

- Tl-Ca-Cu-O: Thallium-Calcium-Copper-Oxygen chemical system.

title: Definition of zero-resistance state
content: The "zero-resistance" state in a superconducting material is defined as the state where electrical resistivity $\rho < 3 \times 10^{-8} \Omega \cdot \mathrm{cm}$, which corresponds to the upper limit of detection based on the sensitivity of the measurement apparatus.

title: Assumption: Chemical simulation of pressure effects
content: The physical effects of pressure on a crystal lattice, specifically the reduction of lattice parameters and the enhancement of mixed valence ratios (such as the $Cu^{+3}/Cu^{+2}$ ratio), can be simulated at ambient pressure through the chemical replacement of constituent elements within the compound.

title: Conjecture: Proposed compound system for high-Tc
content: A superconducting state with an onset transition temperature $T_{c}$ higher than 100 K at ambient pressure may be attainable in compound systems generically represented by the formula $(L_{1-x}M_{x})_{a}A_{b}D_{y}$, where $L, M, A,$ and $D$ represent different chemical elements.

title: Discovery of Superconductivity Above 90 K at Ambient Pressure
content: A stable superconducting transition is observed in a Y-Ba-Cu-O compound system at ambient pressure, characterized by a resistive onset at 93 K, a sharp resistance drop starting at 92 K, and the attainment of a "zero-resistance" state ($\rho < 3 \times 10^{-8} \Omega \cdot \mathrm{cm}$) at 80 K.

title: Phase attribution in La-Ba-Cu-O
content: In the Lanthanum-Barium-Copper-Oxygen (La-Ba-Cu-O) chemical system, the observed superconductivity is attributed to the presence of a phase with the $K_{2}NiF_{4}$ crystal structure.

title: Pressure effect on Tc in La-Ba-Cu-O
content: The application of external pressure to the Lanthanum-Barium-Copper-Oxygen (La-Ba-Cu-O) system enhances the superconducting transition temperature $T_{c}$ at a rate exceeding $10^{-3}$ K bar$^{-1}$ and increases the temperature at which the superconducting transition begins (the onset $T_{c}$).

title: Physical effects of pressure
content: In Lanthanum-Barium-Copper-Oxygen (La-Ba-Cu-O) compounds, the application of external pressure results in a reduction of the lattice parameters and an increase in the ratio of $Cu^{+3}$ to $Cu^{+2}$ valence states.

title: Effect of phase purity on Tc
content: In Lanthanum-Barium-Copper-Oxygen (La-Ba-Cu-O) compounds, increasing the phase purity of the sample relative to the $K_{2}NiF_{4}$ structure—thereby narrowing the superconducting transition width—leads to a decrease in the onset superconducting transition temperature $T_{c}$, while the primary transition temperature remains stable at approximately 37 K.

title: Existence of unstable high-Tc phases
content: Physical signals indicative of superconductivity at temperatures up to 148 K have been observed in unstable material phases within compound systems that contain phases other than or in addition to the $K_{2}NiF_{4}$ crystal structure.

title: Generic compound formula
content: It is proposed that a superconducting state with an onset transition temperature exceeding 100 K is achievable at ambient pressure in compound systems described by the general formula $(L_{1-x}M_{x})_{a}A_{b}D_{y}$, where $L$ is a rare-earth element or Yttrium, $M$ is an alkaline earth, $A$ is Copper, and $D$ is Oxygen.

title: Effect of Annealing on Tl-Ba-Cu-O
content: In the Tl-Ba-Cu-O superconducting system, the process of annealing samples at $450^{\circ}\text{C}$ in an environment of flowing oxygen results in the depression or complete destruction of the superconducting transition.

title: Specific Material Composition and Synthesis
content: The superconducting samples were synthesized via solid-state reaction of $\mathrm{Y}_{2}\mathrm{O}_{3}$, $\mathrm{BaCO}_{3}$, and $\mathrm{CuO}$ with a nominal composition represented by $(\mathrm{Y}_{1-x}\mathrm{Ba}_{x})_{2}\mathrm{CuO}_{4-\delta}$ with $x=0.4$.
