title: Microscopic Critical Resistivity for Localization
content: The microscopic critical resistivity $\rho_{c}$ marking the beginning of electronic localization in a granular system is defined by the relation $\rho_{c}=10L_{0}\hbar/e^{2}$, where $L_{0}$ represents the interatomic distance or the characteristic grain size, $\hbar$ is the reduced Planck constant, and $e$ is the elementary charge.

title: Evidence of Possible High-Tc Superconductivity
content: Polycrystalline samples in the Ba–La–Cu–O system (nominal composition $Ba_{x}La_{5-x}Cu_{5}O_{5(3-y)}$ with $x=1$ and $0.75$) exhibit a resistivity drop of up to three orders of magnitude upon cooling. Specifically, for the sample with $x(Ba)=0.75$, the resistivity measurements provide evidence for bulk superconductivity below $13\text{ K}$ with an onset temperature around $35\text{ K}$, exceeding the highest previously reported $T_c$ of $23.3\text{ K}$ for $Nb_{3}Ge$.

title: Logarithmic Resistivity and Localization
content: In granular films, such as Sn or Ba–La–Cu–O, an approximately logarithmic increase in electrical resistivity $\rho$ observed upon cooling is characteristic of the beginning of electronic localization (the Anderson transition).

title: Microscopic Critical Resistivity Formula
content: The microscopic critical resistivity $\rho_{c}$ for electronic localization in a granular or polycrystalline system is defined by the relation $\rho_{c}=10L_{0}\hbar/e^{2}$, where $L_0$ is the characteristic interatomic distance or grain size, $\hbar$ is the reduced Planck constant, and $e$ is the elementary charge.

title: Josephson Junction Response to Current Density
content: In granular superconducting systems where grains are coupled via Josephson junctions, an increase in current density causes the weaker junctions to switch from a superconducting state to a state of normal resistivity, thereby increasing the bulk resistivity of the material.

title: Phase-Locking Mechanism in Granular Superconductors
content: In a granular system, upon cooling the material below the superconducting critical temperature $T_{c}$ of the individual grains, the Josephson junctions between these grains progressively phase-lock, leading to a significant drop in the bulk electrical resistivity.

title: Identification of Superconducting Onset in Percolative Systems
content: In percolative systems, which include thin films of polycrystals with grain boundaries or mixed-phase systems with interpenetrating grains (such as Li–Ti–O or BaPb$_{1-x}$Bi$_{x}$O$_{3}$), the onset of superconductivity (the start of superconductive cooperative phenomena) is identified as the temperature at which the resistivity peak occurs.

title: Assumption: Validity of Resistivity Peak Assumption
content: The Ba–La–Cu–O system, when prepared as a metallic, oxygen-deficient polycrystalline compound (e.g., $Ba_{x}La_{5-x}Cu_{5}O_{5(3-y)}$), is assumed to behave as a percolative system because it contains multiple phases and grain boundaries, making the identification of the resistivity peak as the onset of superconductivity a valid criterion for this material.

title: Percolative Nature and Granularity of the Transition
content: The observed superconducting transition is identified as percolative and granular in nature. This conclusion is supported by the dependence of the resistivity drop on current density, where higher densities ($0.5\text{ A/cm}^2$) markedly reduce (shift) the onset temperature. Furthermore, the transition is preceded by an approximately logarithmic increase in resistivity upon cooling, interpreted as the onset of localization in grains with an interatomic distance $L_0$ in the range of $100\text{ \AA}$, followed by the progressive phase-locking of Josephson junctions between grains.

title: Synthesis Method of Michel et al.
content: In the Ba-La-Cu-O (Barium-Lanthanum-Copper-Oxygen) chemical system, a single-phase perovskite is synthesized by mixing Lanthanum ($La$) oxides, Copper ($Cu$) oxides, and Barium Carbonate ($BaCO_{3}$) in stoichiometric ratios, followed by an annealing process conducted at a temperature of $1,000\text{ }^{\circ}\text{C}$ in an atmospheric air environment.

title: Composition of High-Temperature Phase
content: Within the Ba-La-Cu-O (Barium-Lanthanum-Copper-Oxygen) system, a layer-type perovskite-like phase exists which becomes unstable and disappears when processing temperatures exceed $1,000\text{ }^{\circ}\text{C}$, leading to the formation of a distinct oxygen-deficient perovskite phase characterized by the chemical formula $La_{3}Ba_{3}Cu_{6}O_{14}$.

title: Chemical Decomposition of Oxalates
content: The thermal decomposition of metal oxalate precursors is characterized by the release of significant quantities of water ($H_{2}O$) and carbon dioxide ($CO_{2}$) gas, a process which generates high chemical reactivity and results in a highly intimate, homogeneous mixture of the constituent metallic components.

title: Coprecipitation Reactants
content: A chemical coprecipitation method for synthesizing Ba-La-Cu-O compounds involves mixing aqueous solutions of Barium ($Ba$), Lanthanum ($La$), and Copper ($Cu$) nitrates with an aqueous solution of oxalic acid, which acts as a precipitant to yield a solid mixture of the corresponding metal oxalates.

title: Synthesis Conditions for the Superconducting Phase
content: The formation of the highly conducting, potentially superconducting phase requires a specific preparation method distinct from prior work on the Ba-La-Cu-O system. The successful protocol involves the coprecipitation of Ba-, La-, and Cu-oxalates followed by solid-state reaction and annealing below $900\text{ }^{\circ}\text{C}$ in a reducing atmosphere ($O_2$ partial pressure of $0.2 \times 10^{-4}\text{ bar}$). Annealing at higher temperatures ($1040\text{ }^{\circ}\text{C}$) or in air results in the disappearance of the conducting phase.

title: Crystal Structure and Lattice Parameters of Tl2Ba2CaCu2O8
content: The single-crystal structure of the high-temperature superconductor $Tl_{2}Ba_{2}CaCu_{2}O_{8}$ (2212 phase) is determined to be body-centred tetragonal in space group $I4/mmm$, with lattice constants $a=3.8550(6)$ \AA\ and $c=29.318(4)$ \AA. The structure comprises double copper–oxygen sheets alternating with double thallium–oxygen layers, with $Ca^{2+}$ cations located between adjacent Cu–O sheets and $Ba^{2+}$ cations located between the Cu–O and Tl–O layers.

title: Atomic Coordination Geometry Definitions
content: In the refined crystal structure of $Tl_{2}Ba_{2}CaCu_{2}O_{8}$ (space group $I4/mmm$), the coordination environments of the metal cations are defined by the local oxygen arrangements: the Copper Cu(1) atoms exhibit a square-planar geometry with four O(1) atoms, supplemented by a weak apical interaction with one O(2) atom forming a square-pyramidal coordination; the Calcium Ca(1) atoms are situated in a cubic coordination environment formed by eight O(1) atoms.

title: Assumption: Structure Refinement Methodology
content: The structural model of $Tl_{2}Ba_{2}CaCu_{2}O_{8}$ is refined using a full-matrix least-squares analysis applied to X-ray diffraction data, employing neutral-atom scattering-factor curves and incorporating anomalous scattering terms for the metal ions (Tl, Ba, Ca, Cu).

title: Atomic Coordination and Bond Lengths
content: In the $Tl_{2}Ba_{2}CaCu_{2}O_{8}$ structure, copper atoms form corner-sharing square-planar $CuO_{4}$ groups oriented parallel to the (001) plane with intralayer Cu–O bond distances of $1.928$ \AA. The copper coordination includes a weakly interacting fifth oxygen atom located $2.70$ \AA\ away, while the Ca ions are coordinated to eight oxygen atoms at a distance of $2.48$ \AA.

title: Method of Structure Refinement
content: In the crystallographic analysis of the $Tl_{2}Ba_{2}CaCu_{2}O_{8}$ phase (space group $I4/mmm$), structure refinement is performed using full-matrix least-squares analysis based on neutral-atom scattering-factor curves. The refinement model incorporates anomalous scattering terms for the metal ions ($Tl$, $Ba$, $Ca$, $Cu$), a term for isotropic secondary extinction, and anisotropic thermal motion parameters for all atoms in the unit cell.

title: Ionic Radii
content: The Shannon ionic radii for the metal cations in the $Tl_{2}Ba_{2}CaCu_{2}O_{8}$ system are defined as follows: $Tl^{3+}$ ($0.98$ \AA), $Ca^{2+}$ ($1.12$ \AA), and $Ba^{2+}$ ($1.42$ \AA).

title: Theoretical Tl-O Bond Length
content: The theoretical $Tl-O$ bond length, calculated as the sum of the Shannon ionic radii for thallium ($0.98$ \AA) and oxygen ($1.30$ \AA), is $2.28$ \AA.

title: Assumption: Metal Atom Occupancy
content: For the purpose of approximating the stoichiometry of the thallium-barium-calcium-copper oxide phase via site multiplier refinement, it is assumed that all available metal atom sites in the $I4/mmm$ structure are fully occupied, specifically to model the observed partial occupancy of $Tl$ on $Ca$ sites.

title: Site Occupancy and Disorder
content: Refinement of the crystal structure indicates partial occupancy of Tl on the Ca site, approximated by the stoichiometry $\mathrm{Tl_{31}Ca_{0.2}Ba_{2}(Ca_{0.9}Ti_{0.1})Cu_{2}O_{8}}$ (where the text implies Tl substitution on Ca). Additionally, the Tl-O layers exhibit disorder characterized by large anisotropic thermal parameters for the oxygen atom O(3), suggesting Tl-deficient sheets where oxygen atoms are statistically positioned to form shorter Tl–O bonds (average $2.48$ \AA).

title: Morphology of Tl-2212
content: Crystals of the thallium-based superconductor $Tl_{2}Ba_{2}CaCu_{2}O_{8}$ are characterized as plate-like particles that do not exhibit a mica-like nature and do not possess a tendency to delaminate into thinner layers.

- $Bi_{2}Sr_{2}CuO_{6}$: Bismuth-strontium-copper oxide (2201 phase)

title: Morphology of Bi-2212
content: The bismuth-based superconducting compound $Bi_{2}Sr_{3-x}Ca_{x}Cu_{2}O_{8}$ possesses a mica-like physical nature, which is associated with a tendency to cleave or delaminate into sheets.

title: Assumption: Cleavage Mechanism
content: In the crystal structure of bismuth-based superconductors such as $Bi_{2}Sr_{3-x}Ca_{x}Cu_{2}O_{8}$, it is assumed that the mechanical cleavage of the crystal between two adjacent bismuth-oxide ($Bi-O$) layers results in the formation of charge-neutral structural segments.

title: Structural Morphology and Inter-sheet Bonding
content: Unlike the mica-like $Bi_{2}Sr_{3-x}Ca_{x}Cu_{2}O_{8}$ compound, $Tl_{2}Ba_{2}CaCu_{2}O_{8}$ crystals do not tend to delaminate. This morphological difference is attributed to the presence of strong inter-sheet Tl–O bonds ($2.0$ \AA) in the thallium compound, in contrast to the weak inter-sheet Bi–O bonds ($\sim3.3$ \AA) in the bismuth analog.

title: Definition of Planarity Metric
content: In the context of copper-oxygen superconducting sheets, the degree of planarity is defined as the distance from the copper atom within the sheet to the average plane formed by the four oxygen atoms within the same sheet.

title: Coordination Geometry in High-Tc Superconductors
content: In high-$T_c$ superconducting oxides containing double $Cu$–$O$ sheets, specifically including $RBa_{2}Cu_{3}O_{7}$ (where $R$ is a rare earth element), $Bi_{2}Sr_{3-x}Ca_{x}Cu_{2}O_{8}$, and $Tl_{2}Ba_{2}CaCu_{2}O_{8}$, each copper atom in the sheet is coordinated by four oxygen atoms in a square-planar-like arrangement and a fifth oxygen atom located at a longer $Cu$–$O$ distance, thereby forming a square pyramidal coordination geometry around the copper atom.

title: Structural Parameters of RBa2Cu3O7
content: For the superconducting phase $RBa_{2}Cu_{3}O_{7}$ (where $R$ is a rare earth element), the distance from the copper atom in the $Cu$–$O$ sheet to the average plane of the four oxygens within that sheet is $0.27$ \AA, and the bond length to the fifth oxygen atom (the apical oxygen forming the square pyramid) perpendicular to the sheets is $2.3$ \AA.

title: Cu-O Sheet Planarity and Correlation with Superconducting Transition
content: The degree of planarity of the copper–oxygen sheets in high-$T_c$ superconductors increases in the order $RBa_{2}Cu_{3}O_{7} < Bi_{2}Sr_{3-x}Ca_{x}Cu_{2}O_{8} < Tl_{2}Ba_{2}CaCu_{2}O_{8}$, as measured by the distance from the copper atom to the average oxygen plane ($0.27$, $0.09$, and $0.03$ \AA, respectively). This increase in planarity correlates with an increase in the length of the fifth Cu–O bond perpendicular to the sheets ($2.3$, $2.5$, and $2.7$ \AA, respectively).

title: Principle of Flux Exclusion
content: Flux exclusion measurements, performed using an alternating current (a.c.) susceptometer to detect the Meissner effect, serve as a diagnostic tool for identifying the presence of superconductivity in a material and for determining its superconducting transition onset temperature ($T_{c}$).

title: Synthesis and Superconducting Transition Temperature
content: Single crystals of the $Tl_{2}Ba_{2}CaCu_{2}O_{8}$ phase, grown from a copper-rich melt with oxides in the molar ratio 2:1:1:3 (Tl:Ba:Ca:Cu) heated to 900–920 $^\circ$C and cooled at 2 $^\circ$C min$^{-1}$, exhibit a sharp superconducting transition with an onset temperature of $T_{c} \approx 110$ K based on flux exclusion measurements.

title: Hubbard Model Hamiltonian
content:
Consider a system of electrons on a lattice near a metal-insulator transition. The system is described by the Hubbard model Hamiltonian $H$, defined as:
$H = -t \sum_{\langle i j \rangle, \sigma} \left( C_{i\sigma}^{\dagger} C_{j\sigma} + C_{j\sigma}^{\dagger} C_{i\sigma} \right) + U \sum_{i} n_{i\uparrow} n_{i\downarrow}$
where $t$ is the hopping integral between nearest-neighbor sites $\langle i j \rangle$, $U$ is the on-site repulsion energy, $C_{i\sigma}^{\dagger}$ and $C_{i\sigma}$ are the creation and annihilation operators for an electron at site $i$ with spin $\sigma \in \{\uparrow, \downarrow\}$, and $n_{i\sigma} = C_{i\sigma}^{\dagger} C_{i\sigma}$ is the particle number operator.

extra.notations:
- $H$: Hamiltonian operator representing the energy of the system
- $t$: Hopping integral representing the kinetic energy scale of electrons moving between adjacent lattice sites
- $\langle i j \rangle$: Summation index over nearest-neighbor lattice sites $i$ and $j$
- $C_{i\sigma}^{\dagger}$: Fermionic creation operator for an electron at site $i$ with spin $\sigma \in \{\uparrow, \downarrow\}$
- $C_{i\sigma}$: Fermionic annihilation operator for an electron at site $i$ with spin $\sigma$
- $h.c.$: Hermitian conjugate
- $U$: On-site Coulomb repulsion energy constant
- $n_{i\sigma}$: Number operator $C_{i\sigma}^{\dagger}C_{i\sigma}$ representing electron occupancy at site $i$ with spin $\sigma$
- $J$: Antiferromagnetic exchange integral
- $b_{i j}^{\dagger}$: Valence bond singlet pair creation operator between nearest-neighbor sites $i$ and $j$
- $\delta$: Doping parameter, defined as the fractional deviation of the electron density from the half-filled state (one electron per site)

title: Definition of Antiferromagnetic Exchange Integral
content:
In the context of the Hubbard model near the metal-insulator transition where $t$ is the hopping integral and $U$ is the on-site repulsion, the antiferromagnetic exchange integral $J$ is defined by the relation:
$J \simeq \frac{4t^2}{U}$

extra.notations:
- $H$: Hamiltonian operator representing the energy of the system
- $t$: Hopping integral representing the kinetic energy scale of electrons moving between adjacent lattice sites
- $\langle i j \rangle$: Summation index over nearest-neighbor lattice sites $i$ and $j$
- $C_{i\sigma}^{\dagger}$: Fermionic creation operator for an electron at site $i$ with spin $\sigma \in \{\uparrow, \downarrow\}$
- $C_{i\sigma}$: Fermionic annihilation operator for an electron at site $i$ with spin $\sigma$
- $h.c.$: Hermitian conjugate
- $U$: On-site Coulomb repulsion energy constant
- $n_{i\sigma}$: Number operator $C_{i\sigma}^{\dagger}C_{i\sigma}$ representing electron occupancy at site $i$ with spin $\sigma$
- $J$: Antiferromagnetic exchange integral
- $b_{i j}^{\dagger}$: Valence bond singlet pair creation operator between nearest-neighbor sites $i$ and $j$
- $\delta$: Doping parameter, defined as the fractional deviation of the electron density from the half-filled state (one electron per site)

title: Definition of Valence Bond Singlet Operator
content:
The valence bond singlet pair creation operator $b_{i j}^{\dagger}$, which creates a singlet state between electrons at nearest-neighbor lattice sites $i$ and $j$, is defined in terms of fermionic creation operators $C_{i\sigma}^{\dagger}$ as:
$b_{i j}^{\dagger} = \frac{1}{\sqrt{2}} (C_{i\uparrow}^{\dagger} C_{j\downarrow}^{\dagger} - C_{i\downarrow}^{\dagger} C_{j\uparrow}^{\dagger})$

extra.notations:
- $H$: Hamiltonian operator representing the energy of the system
- $t$: Hopping integral representing the kinetic energy scale of electrons moving between adjacent lattice sites
- $\langle i j \rangle$: Summation index over nearest-neighbor lattice sites $i$ and $j$
- $C_{i\sigma}^{\dagger}$: Fermionic creation operator for an electron at site $i$ with spin $\sigma \in \{\uparrow, \downarrow\}$
- $C_{i\sigma}$: Fermionic annihilation operator for an electron at site $i$ with spin $\sigma$
- $h.c.$: Hermitian conjugate
- $U$: On-site Coulomb repulsion energy constant
- $n_{i\sigma}$: Number operator $C_{i\sigma}^{\dagger}C_{i\sigma}$ representing electron occupancy at site $i$ with spin $\sigma$
- $J$: Antiferromagnetic exchange integral
- $b_{i j}^{\dagger}$: Valence bond singlet pair creation operator between nearest-neighbor sites $i$ and $j$
- $\delta$: Doping parameter, defined as the fractional deviation of the electron density from the half-filled state (one electron per site)

title: Assumption: Correlated Hopping Approximation
content:
In a doped Hubbard system restricted to a subspace where double occupancy is prohibited, the correlated hopping term $-t \sum_{\sigma} (1 - n_{i, -\sigma}) C_{i\sigma}^{\dagger} C_{j\sigma} (1 - n_{j, -\sigma})$, which accounts for electron motion restricted to singly occupied sites, is approximated as a function of the doping concentration $\delta$ by the expression:
$-t(1 - n_{i, -\sigma}) C_{i\sigma}^{\dagger} C_{j\sigma} (1 - n_{j, -\sigma}) \approx -t \delta C_{i\sigma}^{\dagger} C_{j\sigma}$
where $n_{i, -\sigma}$ is the number operator for spin opposite to $\sigma$ at site $i$.

extra.notations:
- $H$: Hamiltonian operator representing the energy of the system
- $t$: Hopping integral representing the kinetic energy scale of electrons moving between adjacent lattice sites
- $\langle i j \rangle$: Summation index over nearest-neighbor lattice sites $i$ and $j$
- $C_{i\sigma}^{\dagger}$: Fermionic creation operator for an electron at site $i$ with spin $\sigma \in \{\uparrow, \downarrow\}$
- $C_{i\sigma}$: Fermionic annihilation operator for an electron at site $i$ with spin $\sigma$
- $h.c.$: Hermitian conjugate
- $U$: On-site Coulomb repulsion energy constant
- $n_{i\sigma}$: Number operator $C_{i\sigma}^{\dagger}C_{i\sigma}$ representing electron occupancy at site $i$ with spin $\sigma$
- $J$: Antiferromagnetic exchange integral
- $b_{i j}^{\dagger}$: Valence bond singlet pair creation operator between nearest-neighbor sites $i$ and $j$
- $\delta$: Doping parameter, defined as the fractional deviation of the electron density from the half-filled state (one electron per site)

title: Assumption: Definition of Doping Parameter
content: The doping parameter $\delta$ is defined as the fractional difference of the average electron density per site $n$ from the half-filled case (where $n=1$), such that $\delta = |1 - n|$.

title: Effective Mean-Field Hamiltonian for the Doped RVB State
content:
The authors derive an effective mean-field Hamiltonian for the doped Hubbard model in the large $U$ limit. By relaxing the restriction to the singly occupied subspace and approximating the correlated hopping term $-t(1-n_{i,-\sigma})C_{i\sigma}^{\dagger}C_{j\sigma}(1-n_{j,-\sigma})$ as $-t\delta C_{i\sigma}^{\dagger}C_{j\sigma}$, they establish the Hamiltonian in terms of fermion operators and singlet pair operators $b_{ij}^{\dagger}$:
$H = -t\delta\sum_{\langle ij \rangle}(C_{i\sigma}^{\dagger}C_{j\sigma}+h.c.) - J\sum_{\langle ij \rangle}b_{ij}^{\dagger}b_{ij}$
where $\delta$ is the doping concentration, $J \simeq 4t^2/U$ is the antiferromagnetic exchange integral, and the singlet operator is defined as $b_{ij}^{\dagger} = \frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}(C_{i\uparrow}^{\dagger}C_{j\uparrow}^{\dagger}-C_{i\downarrow}^{\dagger}C_{j\downarrow}^{\dagger})$.

extra.notations:
- $H$: Hamiltonian operator representing the energy of the system
- $t$: Hopping integral representing the kinetic energy scale of electrons moving between adjacent lattice sites
- $\langle i j \rangle$: Summation index over nearest-neighbor lattice sites $i$ and $j$
- $C_{i\sigma}^{\dagger}$: Fermionic creation operator for an electron at site $i$ with spin $\sigma \in \{\uparrow, \downarrow\}$
- $C_{i\sigma}$: Fermionic annihilation operator for an electron at site $i$ with spin $\sigma$
- $h.c.$: Hermitian conjugate
- $U$: On-site Coulomb repulsion energy constant
- $n_{i\sigma}$: Number operator $C_{i\sigma}^{\dagger}C_{i\sigma}$ representing electron occupancy at site $i$ with spin $\sigma$
- $J$: Antiferromagnetic exchange integral
- $b_{i j}^{\dagger}$: Valence bond singlet pair creation operator between nearest-neighbor sites $i$ and $j$
- $\delta$: Doping parameter, defined as the fractional deviation of the electron density from the half-filled state (one electron per site)

title: Bose-Einstein Condensation Temperature Formula
content: For a system of bosons with density $n$ and effective mass $m^*$, the Bose-Einstein condensation temperature $T_c$ is determined by the relation $T_c \sim (2\pi/m^*)(n/2.6l)^{2/3}$, where $l$ denotes the characteristic length scale of the system.

extra.notations:
- $T_c$: Bose-Einstein condensation temperature
- $m^*$: Effective mass of a boson or quasiparticle
- $n$: Density of bosons
- $l$: Characteristic length scale (lattice constant)
- $\delta$: Doping parameter (fractional difference of electron density from the half-filled case)
- $k_B$: Boltzmann constant
- $J$: Antiferromagnetic exchange integral

title: Effective Mass of Charged Singlet Pairs
content: In a model of high-temperature superconductivity in doped lanthanum copper oxides, the effective mass $m^*$ associated with the motion of holes, which are modeled as singlet pairs carrying a charge, is of the same order of magnitude as the band electron mass.

extra.notations:
- $T_c$: Bose-Einstein condensation temperature
- $m^*$: Effective mass of a boson or quasiparticle
- $n$: Density of bosons
- $l$: Characteristic length scale (lattice constant)
- $\delta$: Doping parameter (fractional difference of electron density from the half-filled case)
- $k_B$: Boltzmann constant
- $J$: Antiferromagnetic exchange integral

title: Assumption: Dominance of Phase Fluctuations
content: In the limit of low doping concentration, defined as $\delta \ll 1$, the superconducting transition temperature $T_c$ and the superconducting gap are governed by strong phase fluctuations that dominate the system behavior, leading to the failure of standard mean-field theory descriptions.

extra.notations:
- $T_c$: Bose-Einstein condensation temperature
- $m^*$: Effective mass of a boson or quasiparticle
- $n$: Density of bosons
- $l$: Characteristic length scale (lattice constant)
- $\delta$: Doping parameter (fractional difference of electron density from the half-filled case)
- $k_B$: Boltzmann constant
- $J$: Antiferromagnetic exchange integral

title: Assumption: Bosonic Model of Holes
content: In the limit of small doping concentration $\delta$, the system can be modeled as a collection of charged valence bond pair bosons, predicated on the physical view that the motion of holes in the doped resonating valence bond (RVB) state corresponds to the motion of singlet pairs carrying an electric charge.

extra.notations:
- $T_c$: Bose-Einstein condensation temperature
- $m^*$: Effective mass of a boson or quasiparticle
- $n$: Density of bosons
- $l$: Characteristic length scale (lattice constant)
- $\delta$: Doping parameter (fractional difference of electron density from the half-filled case)
- $k_B$: Boltzmann constant
- $J$: Antiferromagnetic exchange integral

title: Superconductivity in the Small Doping Limit
content:
For low doping concentrations ($\delta \ll 1$), the authors propose that mean-field theory fails due to phase fluctuations, and the system behaves as a collection of charged bosonic holes with effective mass $m^*$. In this regime, $T_c$ is determined by the Bose-Einstein condensation temperature of these holons, scaling as:
$T_c \sim \delta^{2/3}$

title: Purity of Starting Materials
content: In the synthesis of the iron-based oxyarsenide superconductor $Sm[O_{1-x}F_{x}]FeAs$, the purities of all starting chemical reagents—including pre-sintered SmAs powder and powders of As, Fe, $Fe_{2}O_{3}$, and $FeF_{2}$—are greater than 99.99%.

- SmAs: samarium arsenide
- As: arsenic
- Fe: iron
- $Fe_{2}O_{3}$: iron(III) oxide
- $FeF_{2}$: iron(II) fluoride
- HP: high-pressure

title: Advantages of High-Pressure Synthesis
content: Compared with the common vacuum quartz tube synthesis method, the high-pressure (HP) synthesis method, conducted at 6 GPa and $1250^{\circ}C$, is more convenient and efficient for the synthesis of gas-releasing compounds like $Sm[O_{1-x}F_{x}]FeAs$ due to its superior super-high pressure-seal capabilities.

- $FeF_{2}$: iron(II) fluoride
- HP: high-pressure

title: Starting reagents for Sm\[$O_{1-x}F_{x}$\]FeAs synthesis (high-purity)
content: The starting materials for the synthesis of the samarium iron oxyarsenide $Sm[O_{1-x}F_{x}]FeAs$ include pre-sintered SmAs powder and powders of elemental arsenic (As), elemental iron (Fe), iron(III) oxide ($Fe_{2}O_{3}$), and iron(II) fluoride ($FeF_{2}$); these reagents are used at purities greater than 99.99%.

refine_history.reasoning: The original parent asserted exclusivity ("consist of") of the listed reagents and added the synthesis context "high-pressure" and "nominal stoichiometric ratio," none of which are supported by Child 1423. Child 1423 only states that the starting reagents include pre-sintered SmAs and powders of As, Fe, $Fe_{2}O_{3}$, and $FeF_{2}$ and that their purities exceed 99.99%. To ensure the parent is entailed by that child, I removed claims of exclusivity, high‑pressure conditions, and nominal stoichiometry, and rephrased "consist of" to "include." I retained the purity statement because it is explicitly asserted by the child. The revised proposition is therefore fully entailed by Child 1423 and contains only claims common to the child.

title: High-Pressure Synthesis of Sm[O_{1-x}F_{x}]FeAs
content: Polycrystalline samples of the iron-based oxyarsenide $Sm[O_{1-x}F_{x}]FeAs$ were synthesized using a high-pressure synthesis method where stoichiometric mixtures of SmAs, As, Fe, $Fe_{2}O_{3}$, and $FeF_{2}$ were sintered at 6 GPa and $1250^{\circ}C$ for two hours in boron nitride crucibles.

title: Crystal Structure of Arsenide Superconductors
content: Arsenide and phosphide superconductors, within the family of transition metal quaternary oxypnictides, form in a tetragonal layered crystal structure belonging to the space group $P4/nmm$, which is characterized by an alternating stacking of $Fe-As$ layers and $Re-O$ layers, where $Re$ denotes a rare earth metal.

extra.notations:
- $P4/nmm$: A specific space group symbol in the tetragonal crystal system.
- $Re$: Rare earth metal elements (e.g., La, Ce, Pr, Nd, Sm).
- $T_{c}$: Superconducting critical temperature.
- $x$: Doping concentration or fractional substitution level.
- $|\delta2\theta|$: The absolute difference between the observed and calculated diffraction angles in x-ray diffraction.

title: Comparison of Covalent Radii
content: The covalent radius of the fluorine atom ($F$) is smaller than the covalent radius of the oxygen atom ($O$).

title: Assumption: Lattice Parameter Calculation Method
content: The lattice parameters $a$ and $c$ for the quaternary oxypnictide samples (such as $Sm[O_{1-x}F_{x}]FeAs$) are determined by applying a least-square fit method to powder x-ray diffraction data, where the refinement converges with a deviation in the diffraction angle of $|\delta2\theta| < 0.01^{\circ}$.

- $x$: Doping concentration or fractional substitution level.
- $|\delta2\theta|$: The absolute difference between the observed and calculated diffraction angles in x-ray diffraction.

title: Lattice Parameters and Structural Shrinkage upon Doping
content: X-ray diffraction analysis establishes that the crystal lattice of the undoped parent compound $SmOFeAs$ has parameters $a = 3.933(5)$ \AA\ and $c = 8.495(4)$ \AA, whereas the superconducting sample with nominal composition $Sm[O_{0.9}F_{0.1}]FeAs$ possesses shrunken lattice parameters of $a = 3.915(4)$ \AA\ and $c = 8.428(7)$ \AA. This shrinkage contrasts with previous reports of lattice expansion by F-doping and is attributed to the smaller covalent radius of fluorine compared to oxygen.

- $Re$: Rare earth metal elements (e.g., La, Ce, Pr, Nd, Sm).
- $T_{c}$: Superconducting critical temperature.
- $x$: Doping concentration or fractional substitution level.
- $|\delta2\theta|$: The absolute difference between the observed and calculated diffraction angles in x-ray diffraction.

title: Crystal Structure of Arsenide Superconductors
content: Arsenide and phosphide superconductors with the general formula $ReOFeAs$ (where $Re$ represents a rare earth metal) form in a tetragonal layered crystal structure belonging to the $P4/nmm$ space group, characterized by the alternating stack of $Fe$-$As$ layers and $Re$-$O$ layers.

title: Theoretical Mechanism for Tc Enhancement
content: In $ReOFeAs$ compounds (where $Re$ is a rare earth metal), a theoretical model suggests that the superconducting critical temperature ($T_{c}$) can be enhanced by increasing the hopping integral of charge carriers, a physical change that is achievable through the shrinkage of the crystal lattice, which acts as internal chemical pressure.

content: The direct current (DC) electrical resistivity of a solid-state sample is measured using the standard four-probe method, which eliminates the influence of contact resistance.

title: Superconducting Transition Temperature via Resistivity
content: Electrical resistivity measurements demonstrate that the $Sm[O_{0.9}F_{0.1}]FeAs$ compound is a superconductor with an onset resistivity transition temperature of 55.0 K and a zero-resistivity temperature of 52.6 K.

title: Assumption: High-Pressure Synthesis Protocol for Sm[O_{1-x}F_{x}]FeAs
content: Samples of the iron-based oxyarsenide $Sm[O_{1-x}F_{x}]FeAs$ are synthesized using a high-pressure (HP) method. In this protocol, pre-sintered $SmAs$ powder is mixed with $As$, $Fe$, $Fe_{2}O_{3}$, and $FeF_{2}$ powders (each with chemical purity greater than 99.99%) according to the nominal stoichiometric ratio required for $Sm[O_{1-x}F_{x}]FeAs$. The mixture is ground, pressed into pellets, sealed within boron nitride crucibles, and sintered in a high-pressure apparatus at a pressure of 6 GPa and a temperature of $1250^{\circ}C$ for a duration of two hours.

title: Assumption: Characterization of Non-Superconducting Impurity Phases
content: In synthesized samples of the $Sm[O_{1-x}F_{x}]FeAs$ system, the secondary impurity phases identified via X-ray diffraction analysis consist of known oxides, arsenides, and fluorides originating from the starting chemical precursors ($SmAs$, $As$, $Fe$, $Fe_{2}O_{3}$, and $FeF_{2}$); these specific impurity phases do not exhibit superconductivity at the temperatures utilized for measuring the superconducting transitions of the main phase.

title: Assumption: Magnetization Measurement Protocol and Conditions
content: The magnetization and dc magnetic susceptibility of $Sm[O_{1-x}F_{x}]FeAs$ samples are measured using a Quantum Design Magnetic Property Measurement System (MPMS) XL-1. Data collection is performed during a warming cycle under a fixed external magnetic field (typically 1 Oe) following two specific cooling procedures: zero field cooling (ZFC), where the sample is cooled to the measurement base temperature in the absence of a magnetic field, and field cooling (FC), where the sample is cooled in the presence of the measurement field.

title: Diamagnetic Transition Characteristics
content: DC magnetic susceptibility measurements (performed at 1 Oe) confirm the bulk superconductivity of the $Sm[O_{0.9}F_{0.1}]FeAs$ phase, showing a Meissner transition onset at 54.6 K, with the 10% and 90% transition points on the zero-field cooling (ZFC) curve at 52 K and 50.6 K, respectively.

title: Relation between dynamical structure factor and susceptibility
content:
For a given wave-vector transfer $\vec{Q}$ and energy transfer $\hbar\omega$, the dynamical structure factor $S(\vec{Q},\hbar\omega)$ is related to the imaginary part of the dynamical susceptibility $\mathrm{Im}\{\chi(\vec{q},\hbar\omega)\}$ via the fluctuation-dissipation theorem expressed as:
\[ S(\vec{Q},\hbar\omega)=\frac{1}{\pi}\frac{1}{1-\exp(-\frac{\hbar\omega}{kT})}\mathrm{Im}\{\chi(\vec{q},\hbar\omega)\} \]
where $k$ is the Boltzmann constant, $T$ is the temperature of the system, and $\vec{q}$ represents the reduced wave-vector.

extra.notations:
- $S(\vec{Q},\hbar\omega)$: dynamical structure factor at wave-vector transfer $\vec{Q}$ and energy transfer $\hbar\omega$.
- $\chi(\vec{q},\hbar\omega)$: dynamical susceptibility at reduced wave-vector $\vec{q}$ and energy transfer $\hbar\omega$.
- $\mathrm{Im}\{\chi(\vec{q},\hbar\omega)\}$: imaginary part of the dynamical susceptibility.
- $k$: Boltzmann constant.
- $T$: absolute temperature.
- $T_c$: superconducting critical temperature.
- $YBa_{2}Cu_{3}O_{6+x}$: Yttrium Barium Copper Oxide chemical formula with oxygen content $6+x$.
- Sr: Strontium.
- Ba: Barium.
- meV: millielectronvolts.
- Bose factor: the term $(1-\exp(-\hbar\omega/kT))^{-1}$.
- Q-scan: a measurement scan in reciprocal space across a specific wave-vector range.
- AF: antiferromagnetic.
- r.l.u.: reciprocal lattice units.

title: Assumption: Effect of Sr substitution on Tc
content: In the $YBa_{2}Cu_{3}O_{6+x}$ crystal system, the substitution of 3% of Barium (Ba) atoms with Strontium (Sr) atoms results in a reduction of the superconducting critical temperature $T_{c}$ by a few Kelvin relative to a pure $YBa_{2}Cu_{3}O_{6+x}$ sample without Sr impurities.

title: Three-component model for nuclear scattering in YBa2Cu3O6+x
content: In inelastic neutron scattering spectra of YBa$_{2}$Cu$_{3}$O$_{6+x}$, the nuclear scattering contribution can be modeled as the sum of three components: (i) a flat background, (ii) a spectral feature peaked near $\hbar\omega\approx 20\ \mathrm{meV}$, and (iii) a broad spectral response across the measured energy range that is described by a damped harmonic oscillator.

refine_history.reasoning: The verification showed a union error: the original parent included an explicit statement that the broad response follows a Bose-factor temperature dependence, but Child 1448 does not assert that temperature dependence. To ensure the parent is entailed by every child, I removed the unsupported Bose-factor temperature-dependence claim and retained only the components that appear in the child: a flat background, a peak near 20 meV, and a broad response described by a damped harmonic oscillator. This revised parent contains only claims in the intersection and is therefore entailed by Child 1448. It also resolves the incorrect subsumption labeling by aligning the parent to what the child actually supports.

extra.notations:
- $S(\vec{Q},\hbar\omega)$: dynamical structure factor at momentum transfer $\vec{Q}$ and energy transfer $\hbar\omega$. 
      - $\chi(\vec{q},\hbar\omega)$: dynamical susceptibility at reduced wave-vector $\vec{q}$ and energy $\hbar\omega$.
      - $\mathrm{Im}\{\chi(\vec{q},\hbar\omega)\}$: imaginary part of the dynamical susceptibility.
      - $Y$Ba$_{2}$Cu$_{3}$O$_{6+x}$: yttrium barium copper oxide with oxygen content $6+x$.

      - meV: millielectronvolt.

title: Assumption: Separation of magnetic and nuclear scattering
content: The magnetic scattering signal in $YBa_{2}Cu_{3}O_{6+x}$ is distinguished from the nuclear scattering contribution by performing Q-scans in reciprocal space across the antiferromagnetic ridge (specifically along the $(1/2, 1/2, l)$ rod in reciprocal lattice units), where the variations in the Q-dependent signal allow for the empirical definition and isolation of the nuclear background.

title: Energy gap in superconducting YBa₂Cu₃O₆.₉₂
content: For the heavily doped superconducting sample $YBa_{2}Cu_{3}O_{6.92}$ ($T_{c} = 91$ K), the spin excitation spectrum at $T = 5$ K exhibits a sharp, resolution-limited energy gap of $E_{G} = 28 \pm 1$ meV, with no sizeable magnetic scattering observed below 25 meV.

title: Relation between Dynamical Structure Factor and Susceptibility
content:
The dynamical structure factor $S(\vec{Q},\hbar\omega)$, representing the probability of a neutron scattering event with momentum transfer $\vec{Q}$ and energy transfer $\hbar\omega$, is related to the imaginary part of the dynamical susceptibility $\chi(\vec{q},\hbar\omega)$ according to the fluctuation-dissipation theorem relation:
\[ S(\vec{Q},\hbar\omega)=\frac{1}{\pi}\frac{1}{1-\exp(-\frac{\hbar\omega}{kT})}\mathrm{Im}\{\chi(\vec{q},\hbar\omega)\} \]
where $k$ is the Boltzmann constant and $T$ is the temperature.

title: Model of Nuclear Scattering Contribution
content: In the inelastic neutron scattering spectrum of $YBa_{2}Cu_{3}O_{6.92}$, the nuclear scattering contribution measured at $\vec{Q} = (1/2, 1/2, 5.2)$ consists of two distinct components superimposed on a constant flat background: (i) a spectral feature peaked around $\hbar\omega = 20$ meV whose intensity decreases slightly with increasing temperature, and (ii) a broad spectral response extending across the measured energy range that follows a damped harmonic oscillator model with characteristic energy $\hbar\omega = 21.5$ meV and damping $\Gamma_{\omega} = 26$ meV.

title: Criterion for Identifying Magnetic Scattering
content: The magnetic origin of an inelastic neutron scattering peak in $YBa_{2}Cu_{3}O_{6+x}$ is established by the observation of a characteristic intensity modulation during a $q$-scan along the $[00l]$ (001) direction, which is the physical signature of antiferromagnetic (AF) phase coupling between the spins in the two adjacent $Cu(2)$ layers within a bilayer unit.

title: Magnetic resonance peak in YBa₂Cu₃O₆.₉₂
content: At $T=5$ K, the magnetic scattering in $YBa_{2}Cu_{3}O_{6.92}$ displays a specific peak around 41 meV that drops rapidly above 45 meV and is characterized by a significantly reduced q-width of $0.18 \pm 0.02$ r.l.u. (compared to the average value of $0.27 \pm 0.02$ r.l.u. elsewhere in the spectrum) and an intensity modulation along the (001) direction.

title: Relation between dynamical structure factor and dynamical susceptibility
content: In the context of inelastic neutron scattering, the dynamical structure factor $S(\vec{Q},\hbar\omega)$ is related to the imaginary part of the dynamical susceptibility $\mathrm{Im}\{\chi(\vec{q},\hbar\omega)\}$ via the fluctuation-dissipation theorem expressed by the relation $S(\vec{Q},\hbar\omega) = \frac{1}{\pi} \frac{1}{1-\exp(-\hbar\omega / k T)} \mathrm{Im}\{\chi(\vec{q},\hbar\omega)\}$, where $T$ is the temperature, $k$ is the Boltzmann constant, and $\hbar\omega$ is the energy transfer.

title: Bose factor temperature dependence of nuclear scattering
content: The scattering intensity of nuclear contributions in an inelastic neutron scattering experiment, specifically those modeled as damped harmonic oscillators, scales with temperature according to the Bose factor correction $(1-\exp(-\hbar\omega/kT))^{-1}$, where $\hbar\omega$ is the energy transfer, $k$ is the Boltzmann constant, and $T$ is the temperature.

title: Magnetic intensity modulation in layered systems
content: Magnetic neutron scattering originating from antiferromagnetic coupling between two $CuO_{2}$ layers (bilayers) in the $YBa_{2}Cu_{3}O_{6+x}$ system exhibits a characteristic intensity modulation in wave-vector scans ($q$-scans) performed along the $[001]$ direction, which is the direction perpendicular to the layers.

title: Nuclear scattering model for $YBa_{2}Cu_{3}O_{6.92}$
content: The nuclear (non-magnetic) contribution to the inelastic neutron scattering in $YBa_{2}Cu_{3}O_{6.92}$ is modeled as a flat background plus two components: (1) a localized spectral contribution centered at an energy transfer of approximately $20\text{ meV}$; and (2) a broad spectral response extending over the measured energy range that follows the Bose population factor and is parameterized by a damped harmonic oscillator with $\hbar\omega = 21.5\text{ meV}$ and $\Gamma_{\omega} = 26\text{ meV}$.

refine_history.reasoning: The original parent asserted an anomalous temperature dependence for the localized ~20 meV component (its intensity slightly decreasing with increasing temperature), but the child does not state or imply that behavior. To ensure every child entails the parent, I removed that unsupported temperature-specific claim and retained only the features present in the child: a flat background plus a localized ~20 meV feature and a broad DHO-like response with $\hbar\omega=21.5\,$meV and $\Gamma_{\omega}=26\,$meV that follows the Bose factor. The revised proposition is self-contained and now reflects only the intersection of the child statements, so each child individually entails it.

title: Temperature dependence of the magnetic resonance
content: In $YBa_{2}Cu_{3}O_{6.92}$, upon increasing the temperature to $T = 81$ K (which is below $T_{c} = 91$ K), the magnetic scattering peak observed at 41 meV at low temperatures shifts to a lower energy of approximately 25 meV, while the energy-integrated intensity remains constant.

title: Relation between Dynamical Structure Factor and Susceptibility
content:
In the context of inelastic neutron scattering, the dynamical structure factor $S(\vec{Q}, \hbar\omega)$ at a given momentum transfer $\vec{Q}$ and energy transfer $\hbar\omega$ is related to the imaginary part of the dynamical susceptibility $\operatorname{Im}\{\chi(\vec{q}, \hbar\omega)\}$ by the fluctuation-dissipation relation:
$S(\vec{Q}, \hbar\omega) = \frac{1}{\pi} \frac{1}{1 - \exp(-\frac{\hbar\omega}{kT})} \operatorname{Im}\{\chi(\vec{q}, \hbar\omega)\}$,
where $k$ is the Boltzmann constant and $T$ is the absolute temperature.

extra.notations:
- $S(\vec{Q}, \hbar\omega)$: Dynamical structure factor at momentum transfer $\vec{Q}$ and energy transfer $\hbar\omega$.
- $\chi(\vec{q}, \hbar\omega)$: Dynamical magnetic susceptibility at wave vector $\vec{q}$ and energy $\hbar\omega$.
- $\operatorname{Im}\{\chi(\vec{q}, \hbar\omega)\}$: Imaginary part of the dynamical magnetic susceptibility.
- $k$: Boltzmann constant.
- $T$: Absolute temperature.
- $\hbar\omega$: Energy transfer.
- $1/T_{1}T$: Spin-lattice relaxation rate normalized by temperature, as measured in Nuclear Magnetic Resonance (NMR).
- $\mathrm{YBa_{2}Cu_{3}O_{6.92}}$: A specific chemical composition of the Y-Ba-Cu-O superconducting system.
- $\Gamma_{\omega}$: Linewidth (damping parameter) of an oscillator.

title: Relation between Susceptibility Slope and NMR Relaxation Rate
content: The slope of the imaginary part of the dynamical susceptibility with respect to energy at the limit of low energy transfers, defined as the ratio $\operatorname{Im}\{\chi(\vec{q}, \hbar\omega)\}/\hbar\omega$, is proportional to the spin-lattice relaxation rate quantity $1/T_{1}T$ obtained from Nuclear Magnetic Resonance (NMR) measurements.

- $\operatorname{Im}\{\chi(\vec{q}, \hbar\omega)\}$: Imaginary part of the dynamical magnetic susceptibility.
- $k$: Boltzmann constant.
- $T$: Absolute temperature.
- $\hbar\omega$: Energy transfer.
- $1/T_{1}T$: Spin-lattice relaxation rate normalized by temperature, as measured in Nuclear Magnetic Resonance (NMR).
- $\mathrm{YBa_{2}Cu_{3}O_{6.92}}$: A specific chemical composition of the Y-Ba-Cu-O superconducting system.
- $\Gamma_{\omega}$: Linewidth (damping parameter) of an oscillator.

title: Assumption: Model of Nuclear Contribution in YBa2Cu3O6.92
content:
In the analysis of inelastic neutron scattering data for the superconducting sample $\mathrm{YBa_{2}Cu_{3}O_{6.92}}$, the nuclear (non-magnetic) contribution to the total scattering intensity is modeled as the sum of a constant background and two distinct components: 
1. A localized spectral contribution centered at an energy of approximately $20\text{ meV}$.
2. A broad spectral response covering the measured energy range that follows the temperature dependence of the Bose factor $(1 - \exp(-\frac{\hbar\omega}{kT}))^{-1}$, which is parameterized as a damped harmonic oscillator with a characteristic energy $\hbar\omega = 21.5\text{ meV}$ and a linewidth $\Gamma_{\omega} = 26\text{ meV}$.

- $\operatorname{Im}\{\chi(\vec{q}, \hbar\omega)\}$: Imaginary part of the dynamical magnetic susceptibility.
- $k$: Boltzmann constant.
- $T$: Absolute temperature.
- $\hbar\omega$: Energy transfer.
- $1/T_{1}T$: Spin-lattice relaxation rate normalized by temperature, as measured in Nuclear Magnetic Resonance (NMR).
- $\mathrm{YBa_{2}Cu_{3}O_{6.92}}$: A specific chemical composition of the Y-Ba-Cu-O superconducting system.
- $\Gamma_{\omega}$: Linewidth (damping parameter) of an oscillator.

title: Pseudo-gap behavior in YBa₂Cu₃O₆.₉₂
content: The temperature dependence of the imaginary part of the dynamical susceptibility $\operatorname{Im}\{\chi(\vec{q},\hbar\omega)\}$ at $\hbar\omega = 10$ meV for $YBa_{2}Cu_{3}O_{6.92}$ exhibits a maximum around $120-130$ K, confirming the opening of a pseudo-gap in the spin excitation spectrum well above $T_{c}$, consistent with NMR $1/T_{1}T$ measurements.

title: Relation between dynamical structure factor and dynamical susceptibility
content: In neutron scattering experiments, the dynamical structure factor $S(\vec{Q},\hbar\omega)$ at momentum transfer $\vec{Q}$ and energy transfer $\hbar\omega$ is related to the imaginary part of the dynamical susceptibility $\mathrm{Im}\{\chi(\vec{q},\hbar\omega)\}$ at temperature $T$ by the fluctuation-dissipation relation $S(\vec{Q},\hbar\omega) = \frac{1}{\pi} \frac{1}{1-\exp(-\hbar\omega / kT)} \mathrm{Im}\{\chi(\vec{q},\hbar\omega)\}$, where $k$ is the Boltzmann constant and $\vec{q}$ is the wave vector.

title: Definition of the energy scale of spin fluctuations
content: The characteristic energy scale $\Gamma_{\omega}$ of a spin fluctuation spectrum is defined as the energy at which the energy spectrum of the spin excitations reaches its maximum value.

title: Relationship between q-width and magnetic correlation length
content: In the analysis of magnetic neutron scattering, the magnetic correlation length $\zeta$ (or $\xi$) is inversely proportional to the width in reciprocal space, $\Delta q$, of the magnetic scattering peaks, such that an increase in $\Delta q$ corresponds to a decrease in the spatial extent of magnetic correlations.

title: Relation between NMR relaxation rate and dynamical susceptibility
content: The Nuclear Magnetic Resonance (NMR) relaxation quantity $1/T_{1}T$, where $T_1$ is the spin-lattice relaxation time and $T$ is the temperature, is proportional to the slope of the imaginary part of the dynamical susceptibility with respect to energy in the low-energy limit, expressed as the limit of $\mathrm{Im}\{\chi(\vec{q},\hbar\omega)\}/\hbar\omega$ as $\hbar\omega$ approaches zero.

title: High-temperature spin dynamics scaling
content: In the high-temperature regime for $YBa_{2}Cu_{3}O_{6.92}$, the energy scale of the spin fluctuation spectrum ($\Gamma_{\omega} = 30$ meV) and the antiferromagnetic correlation length ($\zeta/a \approx 0.84$) remain constant with temperature, while the correlated scattering intensity decreases.

title: Bose factor correction formula
content: The Bose factor correction used to analyze the temperature dependence of neutron scattering intensity is defined by the term $(1-\mathrm{e}^{-\hbar\omega/kT})^{-1}$, where $\hbar\omega$ represents the energy transfer, $k$ is the Boltzmann constant, and $T$ is the absolute temperature.

title: Method for defining nuclear contribution
content: In inelastic neutron scattering experiments on the high-$T_{c}$ superconductor $YBa_{2}Cu_{3}O_{6.92}$, constant energy scans (Q-scans) performed across the antiferromagnetic (AF) ridge at various energy transfers $\hbar\omega$ provide a method to distinguish the magnetic scattering signal from the nuclear contribution, thereby allowing the formal definition of the nuclear background.

title: Two-band Hamiltonian
content: The electronic structure of the copper-oxide planes is modeled by a two-band Hamiltonian $H=H_{0}+H_{1}$ involving $d$ and $p$ orbitals, where $H_{0}=\sum_{\sigma}\varepsilon_{p}p_{\sigma}^{\dagger}p_{\sigma}+\sum_{i\sigma}\varepsilon_{d}d_{i\sigma}^{\dagger}d_{i\sigma}+\sum_{\langle i j\rangle\sigma}V_{i j}\left(d_{i\sigma}^{\dagger}p_{j\sigma}+H.c.\right)$ describes the site energies and $p$-$d$ hybridization, and $H_{1}=\sum_{i}U n_{d i\uparrow}n_{d i\downarrow}$ describes the on-site $d$-$d$ Coulomb interaction. Here, $\varepsilon_{p}$ and $\varepsilon_{d}$ are the site energies, $V_{ij}$ is the hybridization matrix element, $U$ is the on-site interaction strength, and $n_{di\sigma} = d_{i\sigma}^{\dagger}d_{i\sigma}$ is the number operator for $d$ electrons at site $i$ with spin $\sigma$; the charge-transfer energy is defined as $D = \varepsilon_{p} - \varepsilon_{d}$.

extra.notations:
- $H$: Total Hamiltonian operator for the two-band model.
- $H_{0}$: Kinetic and hybridization part of the Hamiltonian.
- $H_{1}$: Interaction part of the Hamiltonian (Coulomb term).
- $p_{j\sigma}^{\dagger}$ ($p_{j\sigma}$): Creation (annihilation) operator for a hole in the $p$ orbital at site $j$ with spin $\sigma$.
- $d_{i\sigma}^{\dagger}$ ($d_{i\sigma}$): Creation (annihilation) operator for a hole in the $d$ orbital at site $i$ with spin $\sigma$.
- $\varepsilon_{p}$: On-site energy of the $p$ orbitals.
- $\varepsilon_{d}$: On-site energy of the $d$ orbitals.
- $V_{ij}$: Hybridization matrix element between $d$ and $p$ orbitals at sites $i$ and $j$.
- $U$: On-site Coulomb interaction energy between $d$ electrons.
- $n_{di\sigma}$: Number operator for $d$ electrons at site $i$ with spin $\sigma$, defined as $d_{i\sigma}^{\dagger}d_{i\sigma}$.
- $D$: Charge-transfer energy, defined as the difference between $p$ and $d$ site energies.
- $\langle \dots \rangle$: Thermodynamic or ground-state expectation value.
- $\Delta_{i\sigma}$: Hartree-Fock order parameter representing the average occupancy of a $d$ orbital at site $i$ with spin $\sigma$.
- $\Delta_{i}$: Hartree-Fock order parameter related to the transverse spin expectation value at site $i$.
- $\langle n_{di} \rangle$: Total $d$-hole count at site $i$.
- $\langle m_{di} \rangle$: Magnitude of the spin at site $i$.
- $\theta_i, \phi_i$: Canting angles of the spin at site $i$ with respect to the quantization axis.
- $E_{tot}$: Total energy of the supercell calculated within the Hartree-Fock approximation.
- $\varepsilon_{af}$: Energy per elementary unit cell for the system at half filling (antiferromagnetic state).
- $\varepsilon_{dl}$: Energy per elementary unit cell in a domain line.
- $N_x, N_y$: Dimensions of the supercell in units of elementary $CuO_2$ cells.
- $N_{dl}$: Number of elementary domain-line unit cells within the supercell.

title: Hartree-Fock Approximation
content: The interaction term $H_{1}=\sum_{i}U n_{d i\uparrow}n_{d i\downarrow}$ of the two-band Hamiltonian is treated within the Hartree-Fock approximation (HFA) as $H^{HF}=U\sum_{i\sigma}\Delta_{i\sigma}n_{d i-\sigma} + U\sum_{i}(\Delta_{i}^* d_{i\uparrow}^{\dagger}d_{i\downarrow} + \Delta_{i} d_{i\downarrow}^{\dagger}d_{i\uparrow}) - U\sum_{i}(\Delta_{i\uparrow}\Delta_{i\downarrow} + |\Delta_{i}|^2)$, where the order parameters are defined by the expectation values $\Delta_{i\sigma}=\langle n_{di\sigma}\rangle$ and $\Delta_{i}=-\langle d_{i\downarrow}^{\dagger}d_{i\uparrow}\rangle$. To account for the $O(3)$ symmetry of the order parameter, these spin expectations are parameterized by the total $d$-hole count $\langle n_{di} \rangle$, the spin magnitude $\langle m_{di} \rangle$, and the canting angles $\theta_i$ and $\phi_i$ according to: $\langle n_{d i\uparrow}\rangle=(\langle n_{d i}\rangle+\langle m_{d i}\rangle\cos\theta_{i})/2$, $\langle n_{d i\downarrow}\rangle=(\langle n_{d i}\rangle-\langle m_{d i}\rangle\cos\theta_{i})/2$, and $\langle d_{i\uparrow}^{\dagger}d_{i\downarrow}\rangle=\sin\theta_{i}(\cos\phi_{i}-i\sin\phi_{i})\langle m_{d i}\rangle/2$.

title: Domain line energy definition
content: The energy per elementary unit cell in a domain line, denoted as $\varepsilon_{dl}$, is calculated using the relation $\varepsilon_{dl}=[E_{tot}-(N_{x}N_{y}-N_{dl})\varepsilon_{af}]/N_{dl}$, where $E_{tot}$ is the total energy of a supercell with dimensions $N_{x} \times N_{y}$ (expressed in elementary unit cells), $N_{dl}$ is the number of elementary unit cells making up the domain line within the supercell, and $\varepsilon_{af}$ is the energy per unit cell of the system in the antiferromagnetic state at half filling.

title: Assumption: Model Parameters
content: For numerical calculations representative of high-$T_c$ superconducting copper-oxide planes, the ratios of the charge-transfer energy $D$, the on-site Coulomb interaction $U$, and the $p$-$d$ hybridization $V$ are fixed at $D/V=1$ and $U/V=4$.

title: Assumption: Boundary Conditions and Size
content: The system is modeled using two-dimensional supercells of up to $10 \times 10$ elementary $CuO_2$ unit cells under periodic boundary conditions, assuming that such supercells are of sufficient size to ensure that the calculated electronic and energetic properties are representative of the infinite system limit.

title: Assumption: Bandlike behavior justification
content: A bandlike (itinerant) description of copper oxides is assumed to be appropriate based on electron spectroscopy data and local-density approximation (LDA) calculations indicating that the ratio of charge-transfer energy to hybridization $D/V \leq 1$, and that for $d$-hole counts in the range $0.5 < \langle n_{d}\rangle < 0.7$, the mass renormalization is small (at most of order 3), resulting in highly dispersive bands near the Fermi level.

title: Striped charged domain-line ground state in doped CuO₂ planes
content: Within a Hartree–Fock mean-field treatment of a two-band model for copper-oxide planes, excess holes form straight charged domain lines (charged Bloch lines) that constitute a striped ground state; straight domain lines are energetically favored over isolated spin bags by ≈0.05$V$ per carrier, and lines oriented along the $(1,0)$ direction are favored over $(1,1)$ by ≈0.005$V$ per carrier.

refine_history.reasoning: The original parent contained specific parameter values ($D/V=1$, $U/V=4$), an SSH-soliton analogy, and the claim of vanishing local magnetic amplitude at the wall, none of which are stated in Child 1483; those extra, interpretive, or parameter-specific assertions made the parent too specific to be entailed by the child. Child 1483 does support the core energetic and orientational claims about straight charged domain lines and a striped ground state (numerical energy differences ≈0.05$V$ and ≈0.005$V$ per carrier) within a Hartree–Fock calculation. The revised parent removes the unsupported parameter values and the SSH/zero-amplitude interpretation, retaining only the substantive, directly supported physical claims and a brief method context so that the single child (1483) now entails the parent.

extra.notations:
- $H^{HF}$: Hartree–Fock mean-field Hamiltonian
      - $V$: scale of the $p$–$d$ hybridization energy used as an energy unit

title: Two-band Hamiltonian
content: The electronic structure of the $CuO_{2}$ planes in high-$T_{c}$ oxides is modeled by a two-band Hamiltonian $H = H_{0} + H_{1}$ involving the oxygen $p$ and copper $d_{x^{2}-y^{2}}$ orbitals. The non-interacting part is defined as $H_{0} = \sum_{\sigma}\varepsilon_{p}p_{\sigma}^{\dagger}p_{\sigma} + \sum_{i\sigma}\varepsilon_{d}d_{i\sigma}^{\dagger}d_{i\sigma} + \sum_{\langle ij \rangle\sigma}V_{ij}(d_{i\sigma}^{\dagger}p_{j\sigma} + p_{j\sigma}^{\dagger}d_{i\sigma})$, where $\varepsilon_{p}$ and $\varepsilon_{d}$ are site energies and $V_{ij}$ is the hybridization. The interaction part is $H_{1} = \sum_{i}U n_{di\uparrow}n_{di\downarrow}$, where $U$ is the on-site Coulomb repulsion on the copper sites. The charge-transfer energy is defined as $D = \varepsilon_{p} - \varepsilon_{d}$.

- $H$: Hamiltonian of the electronic system
- $H_{0}$: Non-interacting part of the Hamiltonian
- $H_{1}$: Interaction part of the Hamiltonian (Coulomb term)
- $H^{HF}$: Hartree-Fock mean-field Hamiltonian
- $p_{\sigma}^{\dagger}, p_{\sigma}$: Creation and annihilation operators for a hole in the oxygen $p$-orbital with spin $\sigma$
- $d_{i\sigma}^{\dagger}, d_{i\sigma}$: Creation and annihilation operators for a hole in the copper $d_{x^{2}-y^{2}}$ orbital at site $i$ with spin $\sigma$
- $\varepsilon_{p}$: On-site energy of the oxygen $p$-orbital
- $\varepsilon_{d}$: On-site energy of the copper $d$-orbital
- $V_{ij}$: Hybridization (hopping) matrix element between copper $d$ and oxygen $p$ orbitals
- $U$: On-site Coulomb repulsion energy at the copper sites
- $n_{di\sigma}$: Number operator for $d$-holes at site $i$ with spin $\sigma$
- $D$: Charge-transfer energy defined as $\varepsilon_{p} - \varepsilon_{d}$
- $\Delta_{i\sigma}$: Mean-field expectation value of the $d$-hole density $\langle n_{di\sigma} \rangle$
- $\theta_{i}, \phi_{i}$: Polar and azimuthal angles defining the local quantization axis (canting) of the spin at site $i$
- $\langle m_{di} \rangle$: Magnitude of the local magnetic moment (spin) at copper site $i$
- $\langle n_{di} \rangle$: Total $d$-hole count at copper site $i$
- $E_{tot}$: Total energy of the supercell
- $\varepsilon_{af}$: Energy per elementary unit cell in the antiferromagnetic state at half-filling
- $N_{x}, N_{y}$: Dimensions of the two-dimensional supercell in units of the elementary $CuO_{2}$ cells
- $N_{dl}$: Number of elementary unit cells participating in a domain line
- $\varepsilon_{dl}$: Energy per elementary unit cell within a domain line

title: Hartree-Fock Approximation
content: Within the Hartree-Fock approximation, the interaction term $H_{1} = \sum_{i}U n_{di\uparrow}n_{di\downarrow}$ of the two-band Hamiltonian is replaced by a mean-field Hamiltonian $H^{HF}$. Using the definitions $\Delta_{i\sigma} = \langle n_{di\sigma} \rangle$ for the spin-resolved densities and $\langle d_{i\uparrow}^{\dagger}d_{i\downarrow} \rangle$ for the transverse spin correlations, the mean-field interaction is expressed such that it allows for local spin canting and amplitude fluctuations, accounting for the $O(3)$ symmetry of the order parameter.

title: Order Parameter Symmetry Decomposition
content:
To preserve the $O(3)$ symmetry of the magnetic order parameter within a mean-field framework, the expectation values of the $d$-hole density and spin components at copper site $i$ are parameterized using the total $d$-hole count $\langle n_{di} \rangle$, the spin magnitude $\langle m_{di} \rangle$, and the canting angles $\theta_{i}$ and $\phi_{i}$ as follows: 
$\langle n_{di\uparrow} \rangle = (\langle n_{di} \rangle + \langle m_{di} \rangle \cos \theta_{i})/2$, 
$\langle n_{di\downarrow} \rangle = (\langle n_{di} \rangle - \langle m_{di} \rangle \cos \theta_{i})/2$, and 
$\langle d_{i\uparrow}^{\dagger}d_{i\downarrow} \rangle = \frac{1}{2}\langle m_{di} \rangle \sin \theta_{i} (\cos \phi_{i} + i \sin \phi_{i})$.

content: For a system modeled on a two-dimensional supercell of dimensions $N_{x} \times N_{y}$ elementary $CuO_{2}$ unit cells, the energy per elementary unit cell in a domain line, denoted as $\varepsilon_{dl}$, is determined by the relation $\varepsilon_{dl} = [E_{tot} - (N_{x}N_{y} - N_{dl})\varepsilon_{af}] / N_{dl}$, where $E_{tot}$ is the total energy of the supercell, $N_{dl}$ is the number of unit cells comprising the domain line, and $\varepsilon_{af}$ is the energy per unit cell of the system in the antiferromagnetic state at half-filling.

title: Energetic stability of the striped phase
content: Numerical evaluation of the ground state energies in large supercells (up to $10 \times 10$) demonstrates that a straight domain line is energetically more stable than an isolated spin bag by approximately $0.05V$ per carrier. Furthermore, the orientation of the domain lines exhibits anisotropy: a line running along the $(1,0)$ direction is more stable than a $(1,1)$ line by $0.005V$ per carrier, leading to a ground state of straight, charged Bloch lines aligned parallel to each other (a "striped phase").

title: Two-Band Hamiltonian Definition
content: The electronic structure of the $CuO_{2}$ planes is modeled by a two-band Hamiltonian $H=H_{0}+H_{1}$. The kinetic and hybridization term is given by $H_{0}=\sum_{\sigma}\varepsilon_{p}p_{\sigma}^{\dagger}p_{\sigma}+\sum_{i\sigma}\varepsilon_{d}d_{i\sigma}^{\dagger}d_{i\sigma}+\sum_{\langle i j\rangle\sigma}V_{ij}\left(d_{i\sigma}^{\dagger}p_{j\sigma}+p_{j\sigma}^{\dagger}d_{i\sigma}\right)$, where $\varepsilon_{p}$ and $\varepsilon_{d}$ are the oxygen and copper site energies respectively, and $V_{ij}$ is the $p-d$ hybridization. The interaction term is $H_{1}=\sum_{i}U n_{d i\uparrow}n_{d i\downarrow}$, where $U$ is the on-site Coulomb repulsion on the copper sites. The charge-transfer energy is defined as $D = \varepsilon_{p} - \varepsilon_{d}$.

title: Hartree-Fock Approximation
content: In the Hartree-Fock approximation (HFA), the interaction term $H_{1}=\sum_{i}U n_{d i\uparrow}n_{d i\downarrow}$ of the Hamiltonian is linearized into a mean-field form $H^{HF}$ that includes terms proportional to the order parameters $\Delta_{i\sigma} = \langle n_{di\sigma} \rangle$ and $\langle d_{i\uparrow}^{\dagger}d_{i\downarrow} \rangle$, where $\Delta_{i\sigma}$ represents the expected hole occupation of the $d$-orbital at site $i$ with spin $\sigma$.

title: Order Parameter Parametrization for O(3) Symmetry
content: To account for the continuous $O(3)$ symmetry of the magnetic order parameter in a two-band model of $CuO_{2}$ planes, the spin and charge expectation values at each copper site $i$ are parametrized as: $\langle n_{d i\uparrow}\rangle=(\langle n_{d i}\rangle+\langle m_{d i}\rangle\cos\theta_{i})/2$, $\langle n_{d i\downarrow}\rangle=(\langle n_{d i}\rangle-\langle m_{d i}\rangle\cos\theta_{i})/2$, and $\langle d_{i\uparrow}^{\dagger}d_{i\downarrow}\rangle=\sin\theta_{i}(\cos\phi_{i}+i\sin\phi_{i})\langle m_{d i}\rangle/2$. Here, $\langle n_{di}\rangle$ is the total hole count on the $d$-orbital at site $i$, $\langle m_{di}\rangle$ is the magnitude of the local spin moment at site $i$, and $\theta_{i}$ and $\phi_{i}$ are the polar and azimuthal canting angles of the spin at site $i$ relative to a global quantization axis.

title: Simulation Parameters
content: The numerical calculations for the electronic structure of the $CuO_{2}$ planes are performed using the following ratios of the charge-transfer energy $D$, the on-site Coulomb interaction $U$, and the $p-d$ hybridization $V$: $D/V=1$ and $U/V=4$.

title: Domain Line Energy Definition
content: The energy per elementary unit cell within a domain line, denoted as $\varepsilon_{dl}$, is calculated using the total energy $E_{tot}$ of a supercell of dimensions $N_{x} \times N_{y}$ containing $N_{dl}$ domain-line unit cells, according to the formula: $\varepsilon_{dl}=[E_{tot}-(N_{x}N_{y}-N_{dl})\varepsilon_{af}]/N_{dl}$. In this expression, $\varepsilon_{af}$ represents the energy per elementary $CuO_{2}$ unit cell in the reference antiferromagnetic state at half filling.

title: Computational Geometry
content: The system representing the $CuO_{2}$ perovskite planes is modeled numerically using two-dimensional supercells with dimensions up to $10 \times 10$ elementary $CuO_{2}$ unit cells, with periodic boundary conditions applied to the supercell boundaries.

title: Electronic structure and midgap states of domain lines
content: The charged domain line phase is electronically characterized by a spin-zero state and the formation of an unoccupied midgap band within the antiferromagnetic gap. The bandwidth of this midgap band depends on the line orientation due to hopping efficiency: the $(1,0)$ orientation yields a bandwidth of $\approx 0.3V$, while the $(1,1)$ orientation yields $\approx 0.1V$. A specific counting rule is established where the number of domain-line unit cells equals the number of charge carriers, and the line is optimally stable when this midgap band is completely filled with excess holes.

title: Two-band Hamiltonian for high-Tc oxides
content: The electronic structure of the copper-oxide planes in high-$T_{c}$ oxides is modeled by a two-band Hamiltonian $H = H_{0} + H_{1}$ involving the $d_{x^{2}-y^{2}}$ copper orbital and the oxygen $p$ orbitals. The non-interacting part is $H_{0} = \sum_{\sigma}\varepsilon_{p}p_{\sigma}^{\dagger}p_{\sigma} + \sum_{i\sigma}\varepsilon_{d}d_{i\sigma}^{\dagger}d_{i\sigma} + \sum_{\langle i j\rangle\sigma}V_{ij}\left(d_{i\sigma}^{\dagger}p_{j\sigma} + p_{j\sigma}^{\dagger}d_{i\sigma}\right)$, where $p_{\sigma}$ and $d_{i\sigma}$ are hole annihilation operators for oxygen and copper sites respectively, $\varepsilon_{p}$ and $\varepsilon_{d}$ are the respective site energies, and $V_{ij}$ is the hybridization. The interaction part is $H_{1} = \sum_{i}U n_{di\uparrow}n_{di\downarrow}$, where $U$ is the on-site Coulomb interaction and $n_{di\sigma}$ is the hole number operator. The charge-transfer energy is defined as $D = \varepsilon_{p} - \varepsilon_{d}$.

- $H_{1}$: Interaction part of the Hamiltonian
- $H^{HF}$: Hartree-Fock approximated interaction Hamiltonian

- $i, j$: Index for copper (Cu) lattice sites
- $\langle i j \rangle$: Summation over nearest-neighbor Cu-O-Cu bonds
- $\varepsilon_{p}$: Energy level of the oxygen $p$ orbital
- $\varepsilon_{d}$: Energy level of the copper $d$ orbital
- $p_{\sigma}, p_{\sigma}^{\dagger}$: Annihilation and creation operators for holes on oxygen sites

- $V_{ij}$: Hybridization matrix element between Cu $d$ and O $p$ orbitals
- $U$: On-site Coulomb interaction energy on copper sites
- $D$: Charge-transfer energy, defined as $D = \varepsilon_{p} - \varepsilon_{d}$
- $n_{di\sigma}$: Number operator for holes with spin $\sigma$ on copper site $i$, $n_{di\sigma} = d_{i\sigma}^{\dagger}d_{i\sigma}$
- $\Delta_{i\sigma}$: Expectation value of the $d$-hole density with spin $\sigma$ at site $i$, $\langle n_{di\sigma} \rangle$
- $\langle n_{di} \rangle$: Total $d$-hole count at site $i$, $\langle n_{di\uparrow} \rangle + \langle n_{di\downarrow} \rangle$
- $\langle m_{di} \rangle$: Magnitude of the local spin at site $i$
- $\theta_{i}, \phi_{i}$: Polar and azimuthal angles (canting angles) of the local spin at site $i$

title: Hartree-Fock Approximation
content: Within the Hartree-Fock approximation (HFA), the interaction Hamiltonian $H_{1} = \sum_{i}U n_{di\uparrow}n_{di\downarrow}$ for a system of copper sites $i$ with on-site Coulomb interaction $U$ is approximated by the mean-field form $H^{HF} = U\sum_{i\sigma}\Delta_{i,-\sigma}n_{di\sigma} - U\sum_{i}\Delta_{i\uparrow}\Delta_{i\downarrow} + U\sum_{i}(\Delta_{i12}d_{i\uparrow}^{\dagger}d_{i\downarrow} + \Delta_{i12}^{*}d_{i\downarrow}^{\dagger}d_{i\uparrow} + |\Delta_{i12}|^{2})$, where the order parameters are defined as the expectation values $\Delta_{i\sigma} = \langle n_{di\sigma} \rangle$ and $\Delta_{i12} = -\langle d_{i\downarrow}^{\dagger}d_{i\uparrow} \rangle$.

title: Spin and Charge Order Parameter parameterization
content: To account for the $O(3)$ symmetry of the order parameter in a copper-oxide system, the local charge and spin expectation values at site $i$ are parameterized using the total $d$-hole count $\langle n_{di} \rangle$, the local spin magnitude $\langle m_{di} \rangle$, and the canting angles $\theta_{i}$ and $\phi_{i}$ such that: the spin-up density is $\langle n_{di\uparrow} \rangle = (\langle n_{di} \rangle + \langle m_{di} \rangle \cos\theta_{i})/2$, the spin-down density is $\langle n_{di\downarrow} \rangle = (\langle n_{di} \rangle - \langle m_{di} \rangle \cos\theta_{i})/2$, and the transverse spin correlation is $\langle d_{i\uparrow}^{\dagger}d_{i\downarrow} \rangle = \frac{1}{2}\langle m_{di} \rangle \sin\theta_{i} e^{i\phi_{i}}$.

title: Assumption: Model Parameters for Superconductors
content: The numerical calculations for modeling the copper-oxide planes are performed using specific dimensionless ratios representative of high-$T_{c}$ superconducting materials: the ratio of the charge-transfer energy $D$ to the hybridization $V$ is set to $D/V = 1$, and the ratio of the on-site Coulomb interaction $U$ to the hybridization $V$ is set to $U/V = 4$.

- $D$: Charge-transfer energy, defined as $D = \varepsilon_{p} - \varepsilon_{d}$
- $n_{di\sigma}$: Number operator for holes with spin $\sigma$ on copper site $i$, $n_{di\sigma} = d_{i\sigma}^{\dagger}d_{i\sigma}$
- $\Delta_{i\sigma}$: Expectation value of the $d$-hole density with spin $\sigma$ at site $i$, $\langle n_{di\sigma} \rangle$
- $\langle n_{di} \rangle$: Total $d$-hole count at site $i$, $\langle n_{di\uparrow} \rangle + \langle n_{di\downarrow} \rangle$
- $\langle m_{di} \rangle$: Magnitude of the local spin at site $i$
- $\theta_{i}, \phi_{i}$: Polar and azimuthal angles (canting angles) of the local spin at site $i$

title: Assumption: Periodic Boundary Conditions
content: For the numerical simulation of copper-oxide planes, the system is modeled using two-dimensional supercells containing up to $10 \times 10$ elementary $CuO_{2}$ unit cells. These supercells are subject to periodic boundary conditions, which are assumed to yield results representative of the behavior of an infinite lattice system.

extra.notations:
- $H$: Total electronic Hamiltonian of the copper-oxide plane
- $H_{0}$: Kinetic and hybridization part of the Hamiltonian
- $H_{1}$: Interaction part of the Hamiltonian
- $H^{HF}$: Hartree-Fock approximated interaction Hamiltonian
- $\sigma$: Spin index ($\uparrow$ or $\downarrow$)
- $i, j$: Index for copper (Cu) lattice sites
- $\langle i j \rangle$: Summation over nearest-neighbor Cu-O-Cu bonds
- $\varepsilon_{p}$: Energy level of the oxygen $p$ orbital
- $\varepsilon_{d}$: Energy level of the copper $d$ orbital
- $p_{\sigma}, p_{\sigma}^{\dagger}$: Annihilation and creation operators for holes on oxygen sites
- $d_{i}, d_{i}^{\dagger}$: Annihilation and creation operators for holes on copper site $i$
- $V_{ij}$: Hybridization matrix element between Cu $d$ and O $p$ orbitals
- $U$: On-site Coulomb interaction energy on copper sites
- $D$: Charge-transfer energy, defined as $D = \varepsilon_{p} - \varepsilon_{d}$
- $n_{di\sigma}$: Number operator for holes with spin $\sigma$ on copper site $i$, $n_{di\sigma} = d_{i\sigma}^{\dagger}d_{i\sigma}$
- $\Delta_{i\sigma}$: Expectation value of the $d$-hole density with spin $\sigma$ at site $i$, $\langle n_{di\sigma} \rangle$
- $\langle n_{di} \rangle$: Total $d$-hole count at site $i$, $\langle n_{di\uparrow} \rangle + \langle n_{di\downarrow} \rangle$
- $\langle m_{di} \rangle$: Magnitude of the local spin at site $i$
- $\theta_{i}, \phi_{i}$: Polar and azimuthal angles (canting angles) of the local spin at site $i$

title: Origin of incommensurate spin modulation
content: The linear relationship between doping and magnetic incommensurability observed in high-$T_c$ oxides is derived from the domain line structure, where the incommensurability $\Delta Q$ is given by $\Delta Q = Q_{0} n_{h}$ ($Q_0$ being the antiferromagnetic wave number and $n_h$ the number of excess holes), corresponding to the real-space periodicity defined by the density of the parallel charged domain lines.

extra.notations:
- $H$: Total electronic Hamiltonian of the copper-oxide plane
- $H_{0}$: Kinetic and hybridization part of the Hamiltonian
- $H_{1}$: Interaction part of the Hamiltonian
- $H^{HF}$: Hartree-Fock approximated interaction Hamiltonian
- $\sigma$: Spin index ($\uparrow$ or $\downarrow$)
- $i, j$: Index for copper (Cu) lattice sites
- $\langle i j \rangle$: Summation over nearest-neighbor Cu-O-Cu bonds
- $\varepsilon_{p}$: Energy level of the oxygen $p$ orbital
- $\varepsilon_{d}$: Energy level of the copper $d$ orbital
- $p_{\sigma}, p_{\sigma}^{\dagger}$: Annihilation and creation operators for holes on oxygen sites
- $d_{i}, d_{i}^{\dagger}$: Annihilation and creation operators for holes on copper site $i$
- $V_{ij}$: Hybridization matrix element between Cu $d$ and O $p$ orbitals
- $U$: On-site Coulomb interaction energy on copper sites
- $D$: Charge-transfer energy, defined as $D = \varepsilon_{p} - \varepsilon_{d}$
- $n_{di\sigma}$: Number operator for holes with spin $\sigma$ on copper site $i$, $n_{di\sigma} = d_{i\sigma}^{\dagger}d_{i\sigma}$
- $\Delta_{i\sigma}$: Expectation value of the $d$-hole density with spin $\sigma$ at site $i$, $\langle n_{di\sigma} \rangle$
- $\langle n_{di} \rangle$: Total $d$-hole count at site $i$, $\langle n_{di\uparrow} \rangle + \langle n_{di\downarrow} \rangle$
- $\langle m_{di} \rangle$: Magnitude of the local spin at site $i$
- $\theta_{i}, \phi_{i}$: Polar and azimuthal angles (canting angles) of the local spin at site $i$

title: AF stability at low doping and superconductivity near the AF–metal boundary in variational studies of CuO2-plane models
content: In microscopic lattice models of the CuO2 plane (including single-band Hubbard and three-band d–p models), numerical variational calculations employing exponential kinetic renormalization of Gutzwiller-projected trial wavefunctions — i.e., trial states of the form ψ_λ = exp(−λ K) P_G ψ_0, where K is the model kinetic operator, P_G is the Gutzwiller projection operator, λ is a real variational parameter, and ψ_0 is a one-particle reference state such as an antiferromagnetic mean-field state or a BCS state — exhibit the following generic behavior: antiferromagnetic order is stabilized at low hole concentration (small hole density x), with the parameter region supporting AF order expanding as the hole density decreases; upon increasing hole concentration or tuning parameters to suppress AF order, superconducting tendencies (or superconducting phases, possibly coexisting with weakened AF order) appear near the boundary between the AF-stabilized region and the paramagnetic/metallic region. This statement summarizes the common qualitative conclusions of both studies without asserting model-specific numerical values or the symmetry of the superconducting order parameter.
keywords: variational Monte Carlo / numerical variational study

extra.notations:
- ψ_λ = exp(−λ K) P_G ψ_0: Trial variational state with kinetic operator K, real variational parameter λ, Gutzwiller projector P_G, and one-particle reference ψ_0.
- P_G: Gutzwiller projection operator (suppresses double occupancy on lattice sites).
- K: Kinetic (hopping) part of the microscopic lattice Hamiltonian.
- x: Hole density (fraction of doped holes per lattice site); "low x" means small hole concentration.
- "AF region": parameter region where antiferromagnetic long-range or strong short-range order is stabilized.
- "AF–metal/paramagnetic boundary": the region in parameter space where AF order is suppressed and a paramagnetic or metallic state appears; superconducting tendencies are often found proximate to this boundary.

title: Non-Fermi-liquid single-particle spectral signatures
content: In strongly correlated electron systems with low-energy collective fluctuations or fractionalized degrees of freedom, the single-particle electron spectral function A(k,ω) (with ω measured from the chemical potential) departs qualitatively from the Landau Fermi-liquid form at low energies and temperatures: (i) A(k,ω) shows a broad incoherent background carrying substantial spectral weight away from any narrow quasiparticle peak; (ii) any quasiparticle peak near the Fermi level has strongly reduced spectral weight Z(k); and (iii) the single-particle decay rate (Im Σ or the spectral peak width Γ) is comparable to or larger than the excitation energy for sufficiently low |ω| or low T, so that coherent Landau quasiparticles are not well-defined.

refine_history.reasoning: The original parent included the stronger claim that the quasiparticle residue Z(k) "can become parametrically small as ω,T→0" (derived in one child via a marginal-Fermi-liquid self-energy). That particular limiting statement is not supported by the other child, which attributes a reduced quasiparticle weight to a separate small parameter (doping) rather than to the ω,T→0 limit. To ensure the parent is entailed by every child, I removed the specific limiting claim and retained only the intersectional, substantive features that both children support: (i) a broad incoherent background carrying substantial spectral weight, (ii) a strongly reduced quasiparticle spectral weight for any peak near the Fermi level, and (iii) a decay rate comparable to or exceeding excitation energies so that Landau quasiparticles are ill-defined. The notation block keeps standard definitions but the content now contains no claims that are supported by only a subset of the children.

extra.notations:
- A(k,ω): single-particle electron spectral function as a function of momentum k and frequency ω (ω measured from the chemical potential).
      - Σ(k,ω): retarded single-particle self-energy; ReΣ and ImΣ denote its real and imaginary parts.
      - Z(k): quasiparticle weight (spectral weight of a quasiparticle peak).
      - E_k: quasiparticle energy (pole of the retarded Green's function).
      - Γ or ImΣ: single-particle decay rate / spectral peak width.

title: SO(5) prediction: gapped π spin-triplet mode in the superconducting phase
content: In the SO(5) superspin description of a system that unifies antiferromagnetic (AF) Néel order and d-wave superconducting (SC) order, the theory predicts a gapped (massive) spin-triplet collective excitation — the "π" triplet — which corresponds to rotations between the AF and SC components of the five-component superspin. This π triplet is a magnetic (spin) mode that appears as a massive excitation in the superconducting phase and is centered at the antiferromagnetic wavevector $Q=(\pi,\pi,\pi)$.

extra.notations:
- $n_a$: five-component (a=1..5) superspin vector that encodes AF and d-wave SC order parameters (AF components correspond to the Néel vector; SC components correspond to the d-wave pairing amplitude).
- $\pi_\alpha$ ($\alpha=1,2,3$): SO(5) generators (operators) that rotate AF and SC components and whose excitations give the "π" triplet.
- $Q$: antiferromagnetic ordering wavevector $Q=(\pi,\pi,\pi)$.
- "gapped" / "massive": the collective excitation has a finite excitation energy (nonzero gap) in the SC phase.
- "superconducting phase (SC)": phase with nonzero d-wave superconducting order parameter.
